'''Cascada''', officially the '''Duchy of Cascada''' (Kǎsùdié Kuwòngsù; 卡速迭公王國), is a nation located in [[Level 107]] and its sublevel, [[Level 107.1]], with smaller territories in [[Level 330.1]], [[Level 79]], and other colder levels of the Backrooms. It has a population of 280,000+, making it the most populous nation. The capital of Cascada and largest city is Piqieng; the Xǖn Piqieng Area is the largest metropolitan area in the Wake, with more than 50,000 inhabitants, while Nomkh is the group's second-largest city and largest economic center. Cascada is divided into 22 provinces, 43 administrative areas, and 7 autonomous regions. Fishing is practiced within the cityscape and Level 107.1 due to their pristine and Cascadan Mawfish-filled drainages and pools, while agriculture is concentrated in the outskirts. The first known habitation of Level 107 dates back thousands of years. Evolution into various different species, with the assistance of CHMV (Cascadan Hominid Mutagenic Virus, of the species ''Gravis inclinationis''), came about before natural selection, alongside the discovery of the Yāpyeon, which effectively cured the virus, leading to Cascadans becoming the only surviving species within Level 107. By the late 2nd millennium BCE, the earliest dynastic states had emerged in the level. The 8th–1st centuries BCE were marked by the unification of many major groups within 107 under the Zhou Dynasty, followed by their breaking out of their authority in the 2nd–3rd centuries. A period beginning in 150 CE, up until its end, marked by the rise of the Kou Dynasty and beginning of the Fusang Period, witnessed the introduction of Joteo by the Sou Shogunates and extensive rule by shogunates, chosenates, and dynasties. During the Fusang Period, most of Cascada was unified under the Kou Dynasty, who oversaw the high point of Cascadan literature and art. A constitutional crisis riddled the Kou Dynasty, ending with its collapse in 1242. The Oregómi Era began after the Kou's collapse; it started with the Long Lost Winter, which was a major cause of the "War of the Lords." The War of the Lords ultimately ended with the emergence of various regional powers, including Xiangzhou, Shenzei, Languo, and the Dominion of Neng. Long peace was instilled onto Level 107, with developments such as the invention of the Nonté Method, the discovery of Level 107.1, and the establishment of the Yunhu Road. The Cascadan states were plunged into a conflict with the Lycheens known as the War of the Eigong, during which a period of cooperation between states to effectively combat the Lycheens, known as the First Unionist Period, paved the way for future collaboration and eventual reunification of Cascada. In 1926, the [[Republic of Aurielle|Persan]] discovered the Cascadan states, first establishing contact with the Xiangzhouans; human technology, such as firearms, was given to the Cascadans and subsequently utilized to fight against the Lycheens. Following the end of the 300-year war, Xiangzhou led a Unionist Restoration Movement; however, economic neglect and land reforms led people to the bloody White Flower Rebellion by people in the outskirts, controlled by Xiangzhou, ultimately forming the Chan Shonun. The Green Leaf Democratic Struggles overthrew many kingdoms in favor of a democratic system, though it left the central authority of various states weak; among these, the Chan experienced a coup that established a dictatorship. Under the leadership of Avedai Fiao, the Chan Shonun, militarized and with ultranationalist policies, invaded Tawagon, initiating the War of 静曜, where the Chan Shonun emerged victorious and controlled all of Cascada. Brutal oppression by the Chan Shonun, alongside depletion of natural resources, was compounded by social unrest and Arisan (later on Auriellen) sponsoring of resistance organizations, leading to the Chan Civil War. By 1977, the resurgent democratic and republican front established control over most of the country, proclaiming 22 independent states, most following some form of democracy. Cascada remained fractured until the 1983 Yanobe Unification Summit, stipulated by Aurielle, proclaimed the Duchy of Cascada. Cascada was initially punctured by uprisings and violence, but by the early 90s the situation stabilized, and Cascada's economy soared to monumental heights, followed by the subsequent rise of mega corporations within the state. The Lustruger Crescen War in Aurielle, and the ensuing occupation of it, including Auriellen territories within Level 107, ushered in efforts of normalization and expansion of trade with other groups, which then transformed into the Territorial Expansion Act. In 1993 Cascada's first outer territory was established in [[Level 330.1]]. In the 2000s, during the Korthie Casiagen War, in which the Cascadans intervened through the Cascadan Expeditionary Force, being the second-most supporter of the Auriellen cause behind the [[Backroom Colonists]], the second outer territory was established in [[Level 79]]; five other external territories would be created following that. In 2020, [[Cascadan Conflict|the AEG invaded Cascada]], triggering sectarian violence upon its end. From 2022 to 2024, Cascada faced another war with the rise and defeat of the Cascadan Black League, which followed Chan Nationalism. Cascada is a constitutional monarchy with a bicameral legislature, the National Assembly. It is the 2nd largest economy in the Backrooms and wields significant power over settled levels, especially [[Level 330.1]] and [[Level 107.1]]; it is considered a great power in global affairs. Cascada is a unitary constitutional monarchy. It is a founding member of VSESA and the Nomkh Economic Forum. Cascada measures highly in democracy, human rights, and religious freedom. The country has been one of the fastest-growing economies and is among the Backroom's largest manufacturers and exporters. ==Etymology== The name Cascada is an exonym derived from the [[Aurielleno]] word for waterfall. The term is based on the Joteo belief of a mythical location created by the god, Haeyato, called Tabù, responsible for Level 107's water supply, and is said to be the exit to [[The Frontrooms]]. The name was adopted as the legal name for the new country during the Yanobe Unification Summit; a direct translation of the name (Kǎsùdié; 卡速迭) was formed to differentiate between the word "waterfall" and the state. ==History== ===Ancient antiquity=== Archaeological evidence suggests that modern humans arrived in Level 107 some 110,000 years ago. The emergence of the Homo sapiens subspecies through the GIG came abroad in two waves, the Biyoufu Wave and the Jiquan Evolution. Cascadans came abroad in the Jiquan Evolution and are the only surviving subspecies to this date, thought to be a byproduct of natural selection and the discovery of the Yāpyeon. The fossilized teeth of ''Kasu Hominids'' and several other species have been discovered in the modern territories of the cities of Saishir, Ishinozhou, and Zejang. The early Cascadans from the Jiquan Evolution initially migrated to the outskirts of Level 107, where they discovered the Yāpyeon plant. Its discovery led to the GIG's eradication for the Cascadan species, but others continued to suffer from it, heavily increasing the possibility of obtaining genetic disorders. Then, early Cascadan tribes reentered the city and pillaged the tribes of the different species, leading to their eventual extinction. ===Five Kingdoms, Seven Dynasties=== Level 107 remained stateless until the late 2nd millennium BCE, when, according to traditional Cascadan historiography, the Yumin kingdom was established. Yumin was followed by the Proto-Five Kingdoms and Seven Dynasties periods, under which the states of Guoming, Senling-Sheng, Zhongdigua, and Haenan occupied a majority of inhabited territory, incorporating the modern lands of Piqieng to Jiangxiang. From them, the Five Kingdoms and Seven Dynasties of Cascada emerged: Guojie Kingdom, Senling Kingdom, Sheng Kingdom, Huangduo Kingdom, Lianyan Kingdom, Kawomaha Dynasty, Yokosunai Dynasty, Kiangke Dynasty, Namsyoeng Dynasty, Gyabnan Dynasty, Beiyo Dynasty, and Jecheon Dynasty. The Guojie and Huangduo Kingdoms and the Kiankge and Jecheon Dynasties were disproportionately sized compared to the others. Jecheon, especially, was a great military power and was notoriously militaristic, often involved in conflicts and the subduement of rebellions both domestically and throughout allied territories. Kiangke followed closely behind in terms of military expertise, though they favored diplomatic expansion and power gainage through treaties, arranged marriages, and allyships that were supported by the dynasty's agricultural production and trade. Guojie and Huangduo were among the smaller of the biggest but still held substantial expertise in innovation and creation. Huangduo, specifically, is said to have thought of the Isami-Shuren farming technique. Throughout the period, the twelve states competed for resources; among those prominent were food and land. As it progressed, the critical defeat of Guojie and Senling by the Kiangke Dynasty during the Chuwon Wars was a pivotal period in the political landscape of the level. The emperor of Kiangke, Hwang Guanting, then led to the Kiangke's territorial expansion through the Hatake Expeditions and the Kiangke's campaign against the Denzing tribes. He also instilled judicial, land, and legal reforms, notably the implementation of laws redistributing lands to be equal for both men and women to increase productivity and the Codified Laws of Hwang. After Hwang's death, a constitutional crisis befell Kiangke. An opportunistic family from the occupied Guojie, called the Zhou, emerged and seized the throne. Widespread revolts quickly rose up as the new authority was deemed fraudulent; brutal oppression produced further civil disobedience, eventually escalating into the Zhou-Kiangke Interregnum. Initial clashes between loyalist forces, concentrated in then-Guojie and Senling, and Kiangke nationalists were brief but turned brutal as the Northern Kangji Offensive by Commander Deng Byung-Chul, a loyalist, began. By 908 BCE, Kiangke nationalists were pushed back following their failed counteroffensive. A nationalist unconditional surrender was signed in the loyalist capital of Guanghai, and they emerged as victorious. ===Zhou Dynasty=== Following the Guanghai Treaty in 892 BCE, a long set of economic reforms and attempted normalization of relations with other kingdoms through alliances and marriages of states. The Zhou Dynasty remained relatively isolationist, even as the Great Lia War began between several dynasties, as it spent its time creating the Daejgon Imperial Library, which promoted education and was an area where a number of notable scholars studied. Philosophy and art flourished, and many commanders and generals moved to the Zhou after the end of the Great Lia War, as many of their homelands were politically and/or economically unstable and did not pay them for their wartime services. The emerging political entity of Haipeng sent its prince to the Zhou's capital to swear allegiance to the imperial family as an act of the beginning of an allyship. The Zhou court accepted and married one of the princesses to the Haipeng prince. The two nations became closely intertwined as all Zhou kings married Haipeng princesses, ultimately leading to the 812 union between Zhou and Haipeng. The capital was moved to Pyonkeng (one of the old names of Piqieng), and the new emperor was multilaterally chosen to be Jietong Zhou. Jietong Zhou led the eastern expansion of the Zhou Dynasty, eventually reaching the outskirts and meeting the Saksukeem and Guiguzie people. The lucrative fertility of the outskirts's lands made a trade route between the two necessary for the dynasty. With the assistance of the Saksukeem and Guiguzie tribes, the rudimentary Wuji Road was established sometime around 790 BCE. Further territorial expansions north were ceased after the Jewang Shogunate invaded them, but advances were halted by Zhou forces, with the assistance of Saksukeem militia who were sent originally to receive training and education. A series of battles culminated in the Fukouna Offensive, leading to the occupation of Jewang. During the Jewang-Zhou War, Saksukeem tactics were brutal and considered uncivilized by Zhou and other states within Level 107. The Zhou Dynasty began investing in the Saksukeem tribes in a process of civilizing, and, in order for instilled political reforms to be more effective, stipulated the union of the tribes into the Saikhet Realm. The competing Yoeng Dynasty took advantage of the economically recovering Zhou and invaded them in 781 and 772. A counterinvasion by the Zhou in 766 conquered the Yoeng. Civil disobedience within Yoeng was a matter of concern for the empire, although oppression managed to disperse activists. A long period of peace followed, lasting around 300 years. In the year 450 BCE, under Lu Zhou, the Zhou Dynasty grew into a massive empire following the invasion of Shen, Xiang, and further conquests into the Dzoneo Hangat. It is agreed that the Cascadan population experienced two growth spurts, one minor from 400-377 BCE and another, more major, one from 200-130 BCE. The Zhou continued their territorial expansion, and by 140 BCE they reached their maximum territorial height following the defeat of the Asanai in the Pulzheo War. The Enlightenment Period began thereafter; Zhou literary and scientific advancements were enriched. ===Fall of the Zhou Dynasty=== In the beginning of the 1st century, the Pink Lotus Rebellion ravaged through the Saikhet Realm, leading to the Saikhet Famine, which also affected the Zhou as they had begun to become increasingly reliant on the Saikhet for food. The ensuing military response to the Pink Lotus Rebellion was an ultimate failure. The death of the sitting emperor fueled internal strife as the government fought for position. The Zhou Constitutional Crisis was particularly hard-hitting, as it inspired various internal rebellions to spring up. Most rebellions were ultimately quelled following the end of the Zhou's constitutional crisis. However, this left the dynasty in a severely weakened state. The following century was much the same; Guiguzie tribes began harassing and pillaging the Wuji Roads and passersby in search of easy supplies, and the situation worsened as the Tokpo disease spread throughout Level 107. The new emperor drafted a national reformation plan to stabilize the falling state of the Zhou Dynasty; however, it was never passed. Their influence in Saikhet, who was now ruled by revolutionaries, dilapidated. Under the last emperor of the Zhou, Myong Zhou, the dynasty lost decisive battles both against the Reformed Saikhet Realm and the Guiguzie Tribes. Myong Zhou's eventual abdication and the beginning of a second constitutional crisis, which remained unresolved, produced the Zhou Dynasty's collapse into various states in the 3rd century. ===Fractured Era and Fusang Period=== The Fractured Era, otherwise known as the first Feudal Era, began in 150 CE and was characterized by the emergence and dominance of ruling chosen people, warriors, and imperial dynasties. Sato Seong, a military leader prior to the Zhou's collapse, established a military dictatorship in Tengdao. The "Gunkedui" administration came to be known as a shogunate after Sato Seong's death due to its similarities. The Xinde Chosenate was the first to formally document the entrance and send expeditions to [[Level 330.1]]; the Lúmen Route was established by the Xinde in 192 CE. Additionally, the first written records of Cascadans by humans appeared during 195-199 CE, many of whom were of East Asian descent that influenced the expeditionaries, who spread their beliefs, culture, and other tenets after their return. Lu Jianhao, a religious prophet and scholar, began preaching Joteo in the Sou Shogunate in 203 CE, under a campaign known as the Long March, where he'd parade around speaking of Haeyato's holy words. The campaign, initially unsuccessful, gained traction and became popular following its official recognition and sanctioning by then-Shogun Kanemaru Seon. Joteo's expansion outside of Sou began during and after the Sou's War in the East as missionaries entered subjugated and annexed lands to spread it. Jianghuan warriors ambushed a passing Kontanan convoy on a trading mission to Tailan. The ensuing, violent reprisals by Kontana escalated into the Bàofuku period. In the aftermath of the Bàofuku period, many of the major dynasties and shogunates were severely weakened, either plagued with civil unrest or economic crisis, or collapsed entirely. The neutral Kou Dynasty, who had avoided conflict, seized the opportunity and launched a campaign to reunify Level 107. By 503, the Kou Dynasty, under Han Kou, had conquered most of Level 107, leaving only a few rivaling states that successfully repelled Kou invasions. A series of intellectual and cultural movements, primarily in the Kou Dynasty but also present within other states, initiated in 823, brought upon a golden era where Cascadan literature and art thrived. The government under Shen Kou devised and implemented the far-reaching Shonijuku Reforms, divided into four major "sectors of importance" per Shen's decision. The Reform began with land reform, based on philosophies shared by smaller Chinese-blooded farming civilizations in Level 330.1, and transcended into political, religious, language, and economic reforms. After his death he was celebrated due to his largely successful reforms; the successor of him ordered the construction of monuments. Newfound interest in Level 330.1 arose in 1032, beginning a race for the colonization of the level. The Khotgat Chosenate was the first to successfully establish a colony in 1042; the Kou Dynasty followed soon after. Communication between the colonies and homeland is an issue that garnered significant attention from scholars and scientists alike—it also led to discourse on the nature of their environment. Various solutions were propagated, ranging from technologies to routes, but few worked. In 1083 the major states within Level 107 abandoned colonization attempts and instead opted for a collective trade state, formed from the various colonies, which would be known as Surzhou. Signs of decadence, mainly through political instability, plagued the Kou Dynasty as early as the 1100s. The ineffective government response to the 1113 Sircu flooding generated controversy and dissatisfaction towards it; numerous incidents followed, which placed the dynasty in a disadvantageous position, mainly the Jinhuan Rebellion and the Grey Noon Crisis, which rivaling nations exploited. By 1223 the Kou failed to stop the secession of two rebelling provinces, leading to the March Revolution in 1242 that toppled the government and unintentionally caused the collapse of the Kou Dynasty. ===Oregómi Era=== Under the leadership of Ming Jie, the Khotgat Chosenate seized the opportunity presented by the chaotic breakup of territories after the Kou Dynasty's collapse. A set of invasions and pro-Khotgat coups ensued from 1244 to 1262, triggering an equivalent response by other states. The resulting rapid invasions, conquests, and colonial-backed coups of Kou secessionist nations came to be known as the Scramble for Kou. A combination of trade collapse, hyperinflation, labor shortages, and colonial extraction stemming from the Scramble for Kou occasioned the Long Lost Winter economic recession occurring throughout Level 107. National fiscal responses to the Long Lost Winter were slow and considered ineffective and mainly consisted of worsening resource extractions within captured territories. The situation entailed revolutionary sentiment largely within colonies but spread to core territories. Level 107's disunity sparked a number of assassinations that created a power vacuum, which was filled by opportunistic nobles, warlords, and widespread violence, chaos, and oppression. The most powerful warlords were those in Xiangzhou, led by Duan Hiro, who controlled the population-rich and territorially expansive capital of Kou, Zejang; the Neng, led by Ki Zhelen, based in northern Thangehay and Serafim; and the Languo, based in Zaishu, among others. Under the War of the Lords, the states often engaged in conflict and battles for territory and hegemony. By the 1340s many events had occurred, such as an attempted restoration of the Kou by Xiangzhouan rebels, the defeat of the Neng in Zoahai, and a power struggle between Xuangzhou and Wonchyongen. Warlords elsewhere, specifically in the southern areas, were divided into various sets of complex alliances, mainly due to their comparatively smaller size and weaker armies. The south saw significantly less action due to fear of an invasion by Duan. In 1349, Chen Quan invaded the Whampo Army in the Gunfendeo War and successfully gained control of Whampo. The Tangdong Group broke over political disagreements, after which infighting between the Kaejin and Qiliakani emerged. By 1362 the political situation in the north had numbed down, with a few large states remaining that cooperated with each other to reconstruct territories and financially support each other to conclude the Long Lost Winter, which had both exacerbated tensions and pressured citizens. Nonetheless, they monitored the southern situation, with Xiangzhou sending warriors to hasten the rise of Chen Quan's state. The era officially came to an end in 1371 after the multilateral peace agreement in Xiangzhou. The Great Reconciliation, which followed the peace settlement in Xiangzhou, was a period of slow but steady economic recovery and rebuilding, although sporadic floodings and colder snowstorms slowed down efforts. Surzhou, who had, for the most part, avoided interaction with Level 107 during the War of the Lords, spread ideals of classical antiquity, humanism, and reform adopted through European wanderers, flaring up interest towards humans. ====Western Miracle and Long Peace==== The 15th century began with a period of record growth throughout Level 107's states, propelling their economies to immense levels that had been previously unseen. The roughly 200-year Pax Pellicius age was initiated, imposing a period of long peace and order—although it still featured numerous internal revolts and external wars. During the beginning of Pax Pellicius, several scholars were sent to Surzhou to study humans, both physically and psychologically; cultural and technological exchanges foreshadowed a cultural renaissance in Cascada in the coming decades. Christianity spread into Cascada through Surzhou missionaries, who themselves adopted the religion from wanderers of Roman descent. Religious persecution of Christians within Level 107 varied but was most severe in Woncheon, Neng, and Quandong. In spite of this, Languo helped establish the first formal church within Panzuo, allowing the expansion of Christianity in Xiangzhou and other states that were religiously neutral or apathetic to it. Individuals such as Duong Min, Tamon Xun, Fan Jie, Pong Ki-Nam, and Otsuka Thu Phuong were leading figures in the process of Cascadan Christianization and its wider acceptance and acknowledgment. Humanist leaders such as Huynh Xuegang and Zhu Heng strove towards the beginning of Cascadan rethinking and reforms in art, architecture, politics, literature, and science. By 1474 Cascada entered a renaissance, with many of its reforms and philosophies following those attained from humans, ranging from intellectuals to peasant farmers. The apogee of the Cascadan Renaissance was between 1484 and 1503, with artists such as Quang ZhenKang, Xu Ping, Riu Moon-He, Sakixu Tanyu, Jia Wenqian, Bai Shirong, and P'ung Yoshida, and inventors such as Gong Yating, Don Qigang, and Han Mai. The first 上教 (Shàngjiào),The 上教 (Shàngjiào) is the head of the Christian church in Cascada. Yil Yotedo, was elected in 1506; many assassination attempts by Woncheon mercenaries followed, but all were unsuccessful. The still active Southern Alliance collapsed in the 1510s after disagreements between members regarding an invasion of Fuxing, which had been thwarted by Yil Yotedo. The Catholic Church continued to function under the Changging doctrine, preferring to mediate between the level's powers to secure peace; however, they could not stop the Neng Religious Wars and the Struggle of the Cross, which marked the end of the Renaissance—although the Long Peace continued. Languo launched a series of legal and constitutional reforms ordered by Chaejin that grew to become very unpopular with his subjects. Zhel Langzhe, a notorious warlord, mounted a revolt in western Languo; Zhel's rebellion quickly escalated into an open civil war, and Chaejin proved ineffective in dealing with him. By 1532 Zhel had conquered most of western Languo except for its major cities such as Qingzuishan and Luohe. Zhel attempted to forcefully take Qingzuishan, but this led to his death and the subsequent suppression of the revolt. Nevertheless, the inability to deal properly with Zhel led to Chaejin's abdication. The new imperial administration under Dàn Dyeong-Che drafted a new constitution that held considerably liberal elements. Great development in the fields of exploration and technology continued even after the Renaissance had passed. The antiquated Wuji Road was replaced by the Yunhu Road in 1545. In the 1553 Peasant Revolt at Cheojin, a royal army formation led by Lu Ten unintentionally discovered [[Level 107.1]] after taking what was a mislabeled underground shortcut. The discovery propagated quickly and led to numerous other entrances, such as those in Jarkim and Kiangke, being found as well. The cities near Level 107.1 benefited from access rights and commercial activity. In the same year, Researcher Chiozin Se of Xiangzhou invented a way to effectively combat disease through phage therapy, which was named the Nonté Method; the Nonté Method was tested in the 1571 Ketriqianinnig Plague. ===Post-Rennaisance, Eigong Period=== The Cascadan Renaissance came to an end with the Thirty Years' War and the Sack of Zejang. Foreshadowing to the conflict came with political tensions between Xiangzhou and Languo, which were propelled by the ascension of Li Hulfeng as king and the assassination of religious leader Shao Hu in Languon territory that led to a bitter rivalry between Li Hulfeng and Queen Yao Zeming. Both sides sent spies in attempts to undermine each other; a spy war ensued. The tensions culminated in the aforementioned conflict, bringing devastation to most of Level 107; the battleground was even. The Languons initially held a stronghold within various Xiangzhouan cities and settlements, and they eventually managed to reach the capital of Zejang; however, by 1592, Xiangzhou mounted a counteroffensive that recaptured most of their lost lands and breached Languo defensive positions in their land. Peace was reached with the 1604 Peace Agreement in Wonsong. Revolution swept through Xiangzhou and other states with the Equalist Spring, incorporating various events that led to the toppling, or attempted toppling, of kings and leaders. The most significant events during the Equalist Spring were the Hulfeng Scandal, the Languo coup d'état, the religious uprising in Quandong, and the protests in Peony. Zeng Zexian, having successfully led the transnational Spring March, initiated the Harmony Restoration Movement at the end of the Equalist Spring. The brief peace instilled in Level 107 was broken by the territorial dispute with the [[Lystol States|Lycheens]] and Surzhou throughout the 1670s. The aggressive policies of Jinpa, the Surzhouan leader, fueled by total support from the nations within Level 107, ignited the ethnic conflict between the Cascadans and Lycheens in 1682. The first quarter-century of the 300-year war was catalyzed by defined offensives and battles; the birth of the Confederation of Zuhoru was the result of the efforts of Xiangzhouan nationalists and monarchists to establish a united front to effectively combat the Lycheen threat during this phase of the conflict, and it also paved the way for future collaboration between the Cascadan states. In 1707, the Cascadans and Lycheens signed an armistice in Dien Long, Surzhou. A central institution to criminally prosecute war criminals was created after the armistice was signed; however, it was considered ineffective in its judicial tasks and became tasked with gathering information on them. Records from the Taisengel Commission indicated that the initial periods of the war involved high levels of species cleansing, arson, perfidy, and attacks on civilian infrastructure. By 1711, fighting resumed; however, it transitioned into a period known as "Scattered War," which focused on raids, starvation, and otherwise less obvious combat, though nonetheless the war was considered extended by diplomatic irregularity. In spite of it, throughout the 18th century significant advancements in medicine, such as rudimentary antidotes, and weaponry, were accomplished, largely attributed to the War in the Eigong. A brief economic stagnation in 1745 somewhat impaired the Zuhoru's war-fighting capabilities. {{Blurimage|caption=A Persan soldier observing a forest fire caused by Cascadans.|file=Persan fires.png}} An expeditionary team led by [[Republic of Aurielle|Persan]] explorer Marcus Agosti discovered Level 107 on March 12th, 1926, during the Seven Voyages expeditions. Persan-Zuhoru trade relations began soon afterwards when the Huininco Route was founded in October, bringing a flow of resources and human technologies that surpassed at-the-time Cascadan ones, the most significant of which being rifles, in exchange for fur, valuable minerals, and rights to examine the Cascadan species. Exchanged rifles were quickly used by Zuhoru in combat. By 1931 a major offensive was mounted by the Cascadans that practically ended the War of the Eigong; diplomatically, an unconditional surrender was signed in Haesong after the Kou Offensive. ===Post-Eigong=== The peaceful political collapse of the Zuhoru Confederation opened an era of industrialization under the name of Shènjuéqǐ. The successes of Chosutojen (restructuring) within Shenzei and Shizi forwarded the wide appearance of economic liberalism and also accelerated the embarkment of the Unionist Restoration MovementIt involved the unification or subjugation of minor states with Xiangzhou to form a Pan-Zuhoru State. by Xiangzhouan leader Ruihua Fu. In Shizi, Gonguo, and Neng, religious zealot agitation culminated in the insurgency of the 權雪洛 (Xuěló Erkàng), a militant reactionary organization that sought to instate a theocratic realm under Joteo and create a secret society conspiring for the overthrow of multiple states. A five-year counterinsurgency campaign significantly subdued both the insurgency and the secret society; however, remaining holdouts continued to operate until the 1960s. External shocks, mainly the developing Gimhae trade war, pushed the northern economies into stagnation. The sister states of Shizi and Shenzei were the first to stabilize their economies in 1949. Other states followed suit, but their economic stability was questionable. In Xiangzhou, the Unionist Restoration Movement was successful and united the eight nations in the Danghae area, including the outskirts territory of Meixing, under the Xiangzhouan Federated States, who functioned within the framework of a democratic system. The XFS quickly solidified itself as a great power within Level 107, being at the forefront of the level's representation in international politics and imagery. In 1954 the Prime Minister Jia Shuren became increasingly concerned about the representative equity of the eight states composing the union and thus ratified the 1955 Xiangzhouan Constitution. Xiangzhouan conservatives became increasingly dissatisfied with the new constitution, as they saw it as conceding to minority nationalists. This created tensions within the unionist leadership and led to protests and strikes within Xiangzhou. Throughout the late 1950s, with the election of Yòuzǐ Seumiseu, a Xiangzhouan nationalist, ethnic tensions grew significantly. Seumiseu's administration severely undermined Shuren's progress on representative equity, and it was often that Seumiseu attempted to circumvent the 1955 constitution, although he continuously failed. Seumiseu primarily stayed influential through appeasing the majority while simultaneously undermining minority issues; calls for his impeachment due to it were often made; however, their success was lackluster. In 1959 Seumiseu drafted ill-fated land reforms that were scrutinized and validated by ministries with little real experience in farming. Meixing, the XFS's main agricultural state, was deeply impacted by it; the situation was further worsened by the Huanse River flood that destroyed many of the crops. A famine followed that was dealt with by the local government due to ineffective central government assistance. Protests, which were quickly suppressed by security forces, swiftly swept through the XFS. Languo launched peacekeeping operations in the XFS; peacekeeping garrisons, however, were attacked by XFS security forces, which infuriated the regional community, leading to a set of sanctions being imposed. The local Meixing population, disgruntled by years of oppression, revolted and declared a free state. The denominated White Flower Rebellion was the biggest and bloodiest military and political failure of Yòuzǐ Seumiseu. As Meixing declared independence under the Chan Shonun, the Yeowon and Xiangzhouan forces indulged in a violent and perilous rivalry that ended with the assassination of Seumiseu and the violent implosion of the Federated States. The assassination of Yòuzǐ Seumiseu inspired the Green Leaf Democratic Struggle, a wave of pro-democracy mass protests in kingdoms or otherwise non-democratic nations throughout Level 107. The catalysts of the GLDS were the toppling of Dae-Gi's regime in Anjin, the death of Liang Seison, and the transnational worker strike. Various regimes fell, and others went through constitutional reforms that established democratic systems; however, many of these new regimes' central governments were severely weakened and had difficulties controlling their territories and enforcing their laws. The Chan Shonun's, one of such cases, instability, however, was broken by the Zhejon Coup, which established the dictatorial Avedai Fiao as leader of the Chan Shonun. ===Chani Era=== The Chan Shonun under Avedai Fiao adopted the ideology of Chan Nationalism, a Cascadan ultranationalist ideology that believed that Cascadans were the superior race and aimed to cleanse the Backrooms of non-Cascadans to spread their species—the ideology was partly inspired by Nazism. Avedai corroborated chauvinist policies, started a period of mass militarization, and began a campaign of radicalization and weaponization to conduct state-sponsored mass murders and kidnappings against Humans and state-sponsored pogroms to vandalize and loot the shops, property, and religious buildings belonging to Humans, People of Non-Jotean Religions, and Non-Chani Cascadans. Under the directive of Ryou Xue, the first Chan concentration camp was established in Shozuwang. Soon after, the Chan military launched a surprise invasion of Tawagon on March 2, 1962, sparking the War of 静曜 (Yìng Réikōe), which continued until 1969. The attack occurred in the early morning with two groups—the North and South Groups—leading the attacks on their respective areas of operation. North Group's targets were from Huiju to Narayako, while South Group's targets were from Tariahoroot to Narayako. Both attacks were timed during regular maintenance periods, when the Tawagon's military ground enemy early warning detection systems were momentarily disabled. The North Group initiated the operation with an intense artillery bombardment accompanied by a simultaneous wave of infantry forces. The Chani Revolutionary Army (CRA) functioned under the doctrine of overwhelming firepower, that worked well for the relatively underprepared and underpaid Tawagon military. By April, both the North and South groups met in Narayako, and an unconditional surrender was signed; Tawagon was integrated as a state into the Chan Shonun. After the capture and capitulation of Tawagon, CRA forces flooded the area and began killing several hundred Tawagonians and forced tens of thousands to flee; they mainly targeted those with supposed allegiances to the government and military as a preemptive measure to stop the creation of partisan groups. The invasion of Tawagon was met with expected condemnation from the various groups; Chani officials began preparations for war to be declared. General Xei Yoham of the CRA began to become increasingly interested in the development of mechanized and armored vehicles for units and was eventually placed as overseer of the Chan's new mechanized vehicles program, which primarily focused on APCs and its stronger variant, the IFV—then called the APFV. {{Blurimage|caption=The view from the periscope of an early APFV model.|file=Vehicle testing.png}} On 6 May 1962, Avedai Fiao met with Miluo Xinhuilang in Nogoongas, Chan, where they reached an agreement for a bilateral military and cooperative alliance. Despite strong external pressure, they followed and implemented the deal on May 10th. In August, the Chani-Ximaoji alliance started Operation 鍛壳煒 (Zhō tou ngè)red bullet against the Tao, Wudong, and Ziyoudi forces. Operation 鍛壳煒 began on August 15, when the 2nd Motorized Brigade of the Chan Shonun, aided by Ximaojin infantry from Kurimamoto, began the offensive near Hengping, Tao. The CRA captured most of Tao by September, Wudong by October, and Ziyoudi, which resisted the most, by January 1963. Despite wishes to continue invasions by Miluo Xinhuilang, other states had proliferated their military capabilities much farther than official estimates placed them to be. The Chani-Ximaoji put forth Plan 931, commencing a period of terror war aimed at the incapacitation of the enemy state's capabilities. Chani and Ximaoji-backed terrorist networks and lone wolves accomplished terrorist attacks, kidnappings, and hostage situations, the most prominent of which were the 1963 Pingxing Attack and the Jiaotou Hostage Crisis. Many of the attacks were aimed at manufacturing and military installations. August saw the declaration of total war on Cascada by the Chani government; many underground pro-Chani networks were activated and began actively rebelling against their government, leading to a declaration of emergency in three of the four affected states. The CRA then staged an attack on border positions to justify the invasions of Yeowon, Mizhi, Guxang, and Ji. In the first weeks of the concomitant invasion of the four nations, the CRA and Army of the Ximaoji Realm (ATXR) attacked headquarters of Mizhi and Yeowon forward elements. After three days of fighting, the outnumbered Yeowonian forces were defeated. Mizhian forces held for longer and fared considerably better but still had to retreat after a week of fighting. The ATXR-CRA offensive continued and was most concentrated on Yeowon, the most major of the four, which was costly for them. Reactions to the situation were harsher than before; however, the other nations could agree on very little. Some proposed sending peacekeeping forces to stagnate the conflict, to subsequently set up a demilitarized Chani Ximaoji-Yeowon, Mizhi, Guxang, and Ji border stretching through what would be called the 24th Line; others proposed outright intervention and deposition of Avedai Fiao; and many proposed harsh sanctions on the Chan Shonun and the Ximaoji Realm to cripple their economies and effectively eliminate their war-fighting capabilities, which from a superficial standpoint were already stretched thin and were mainly run by workers from invaded territories that continued production due to fearmongering. The overarching sentiment was for sanctions and embargos; the nations that proposed intervention or peacekeeping backed down due to not holding enough international weight to be effectively put into action. Chan had expected this and years prior, it had accumulated major central bank and foreign exchange reserves, sanction proofing, and an overall shift to a wartime economy. Chan also began weapon smuggling to circumvent the arm embargos. Ximaoji did not fare as well. Even with the Chan's slightly damaged economy, it continued offensive operations in Yeowon. Elsewhere, pro-Chani groups managed to coup the government in Guxang and establish a friendly Chani administration under Kae Zexian, which was not greeted well by the local populace, who resisted Zexian's regime. In December the Battle of Jyusho began. Yeowon's military strategy proved increasingly unstable and failed to defend the nation, which greatly affected their morale; the CRA dropped propaganda leaflets on Yeowonian positions suggesting they defect. It was wildly successful—the issue of mass defection became unfixable among Yeowon's ranks, directly crippling their abilities in properly defending Jyusho. Yeowonian troops entrenched themselves within the city and held their ground against the Chani mechanized assaults. Jyusho was devastated; many of the buildings were destroyed. Some Yeowonians bunkered in their homes; others fled en masse. Following their defeat in Jyusho, Yeowon capitulated. Soon after the capitulation of Yeowon, forces relocated to fight against Mizhi; the nation was effectively surrounded; however, its morale and fighting spirit remained at an all-time high. By January the Mizhian Ground Defense Force (MGDF) mounted a successful counterattack in Ji and the city of Bulmani; the denonimed Operation 麗岩 (fǔa puhǔyen) resulted in Mizhi recapturing approximately a total of 181 square kilometers of territory and the first major defeat for the Chani military. In spite of successes, the operation did little in terms of pushing the tides of the war; by March, Ji had fallen to Chani and Ximaoji hands, and most of Mizhi was captured. MGDF positions were reduced to isolated positions around Zoatong, Panyuan, and Noncho, holding for a bloody two months before being defeated. By then, many MGDF personnel had formed militias or escaped to bordering nations, mainly Xiangzhou. The Chan government made an educated assumption that they could not defeat Xiangzhou in a war, and therefore decided to allocate their military power in the invasion of sorrounding, significantly weaker states. During the period in between the defeat of Mizhi and the planned invasion of the Sozin area states, Chani farmer Hu Qiu discovered the Flatlands and subsequently the helicopters within it. An envoy transmitted the information to the Chani government, who, initially negligent of the discovery, sent expeditionary teams after they had been informed of the helicopter's military equipment. The expeditionary team learned of the helicopter's ability to fly while testing out the frontal flight controls; the first air assault teams were subsequently formed, which were trained by former West German Luftwaffe pilot Alfred Wachsmuth, who was conditionally released from prison under the circumstance. The recently formed Chani Aviation Brigade was first deployed in the invasion of the Sozin area, first seeing combat in their landing in Hoeryong on July 13, which was a resounding success due to the anti-aircraft technology being practically non-existent during the decade. The Aviation Brigade was then used extensively in the wider Sozin campaign as a bridgehead and for hit-and-run attacks. By October the Sozin campaign had finalized, its quick ending occurring due to the Sozin nation's still experiencing instability and weakened coordinating abilities. The issue of oil shortages became an increasingly prevalent issue within the military, especially with the resource-intensive tactics of the CRA. It forced Avedai Fiao to focus interest on [[Level 340]] with the intent of setting up proxies for resource extraction. The Chani government embarked on an unwilling diplomatic campaign, leading to the reluctant establishment of formal diplomatic ties with the Brevalasemjke and the Gosudarstvo Antonskyomo. The Rashinaramanis Route was promptly founded to ensure the steady flow of resources; however, it was consistently harassed by militias and freedom-fighting groups. Xiangzhou preemptively struck the Chan Shonun in early 1964 due to fears of them becoming the last remaining holdout within Level 107, at which point they would not be able to defeat them. The Xiangzhouan High Command under Hiruma Hyuk organized a large training near the Xiangzhouan-Chani border, which would transform into a surprise invasion aimed at separating the bulk of the CRA—who were still in the Sozin area—from mainland Chan territory, supported by anti-Chani militias established along occupied territory. The preemptive strike began with a push from the border settlement of Hoerneung to the Miguozhi city of Tokaypem and a simultaneous southward offensive alongside northern skirmishes aimed at preoccupying CRA forces. {{Blurimage|caption=CRA Bell UH-1 lifting up before going to support the Spring Offensive.|file=Gyeng.png|align=left}} Along the Aridamato-Garaisoy line, General Hong Linh commanded the 3rd Army Detachment to entrap and break apart ATXR forces set to stay behind; he was accompanied by partisans who despised the Chanis and Ximaoji for their actions in their country. Throughout the early months of the attack, most Xiangzhouan objectives were achieved; ATXR forces were entrapped in the Makaunon state, while CRA forces were being bogged down northward. However, Xiangzhou failed to fully encircle Chan forces and properly cripple logistic routes. By spring of 1964, the ATXR and CRA renewed their offensive efforts after successfully stabilizing the frontlines, which had previously been concentrated in the streets of Tangxing and Liaozihua. A subsequent breakthrough in the northern portion of Liaozihua by the 12th Brigade culminated in the Spring Offensive; vehicles were extensively used, but they were not as impervious as before due to the development of anti-tank technology in anticipation of the attack; rather, helicopters proved to be more challenging to deal with. The Battle of Honmatukum was noted as the first widespread deployment of air assault units in support of a combined-arms operation. Xiangzhou learned valuable lessons from their bloody victory in the city, particularly in how to deal with airmobile troops. Two tactics stemming from the battle were developed: gift wrapping, consisting of deliberately giving up territory up to where airmobile forces would be deployed, and killzoning, involving the deployment of a rapid response force to counterattack. By the end of the Spring Offensive, the front had significantly stagnated. The last offensive operations occurred in early to mid-autumn, mainly attacking the south and east. As winter began, both sides refrained from any large-scale actions, and the situation was relatively stable. Resumption of hostilities by the CRA came about during the start of autumn, but both armies became locked in a hurling stalemate, lasting much of the Xiangzhouan front. 1965 opened with an invasion from the north by the armies of Mudanqihar, Zhonping, Cheonhwa, and Choryun on the side of Xiangzhou, spearheading an offensive into the Seosu. Chani positions scrambled to cover their northern flanks, committing newly trained forces to the north, depriving those in the Xiangzhouan front of reinforcements, who faced increasingly violent and hardened offensive efforts by the Xiangzhouans. Avedai Fiao and her inner circle were placed in a difficult position, though her military advisors noted that the economies of the new belligerents were still in a process of slow economic recovery from their economic stagnation in 1949, therefore making war questionable among their respective populaces. Chani People's Revolutionary Guard (CPRG) were tasked with waging economic warfare and crippling supply lines. Meanwhile, more troops were being poured in by the CRA to stabilize the north, while ATXR and allied militias were called to support CRA forces in Xiangzhou. Several localized breakthroughs by airmobile forces in the Mudanqiharian front indicated a lack of communication between allied forces, and within days the CRA and ATXR conducted successful counteroffensive operations before being halted by defensive lines in the gates of Zhonghuan. The Chan Shonun sponsored anti-war uprisings in Zhonping and emboldened the CPRG to further attack industrial installations. Cheonhwa and Mudanqihar's economic output was crippled by CPRG action and artillery strikes, while Chani sponsoring culminated in the Polyente Uprising, which overthrew President Quyen Zhihao and established radical pro-Chani Tongbang Pyong-Chong as leader. Pyong-Chong pursued a separate peace deal with the Chan Shonun, wherein Zhonping surrendered and joined the Chan-Ximaoji alliance. With the pacification of the north, the CRA was allowed to redirect more resources to relief forces in the Xiangzhouan front, who came close to breaking through exhausted defensive lines. Tongbang Pyong-Chong sent a communique to the Chanis, requesting authorization to deploy forces in support of the CRA. The Chani Supreme Council debated over the offer; some argued that Pyong-Chong should focus on pacifying rebelling subjects, while others argued that additional forces could only help the war effort. The latter was accepted. Mudanqihar troops joined CRA forces in the Xiangzhouan front. Defeating Xiangzhou, even after years of fighting, remained a long and arduous undertaking. The Xiangzhouans refused to surrender, despite the fact that they were practically alone aside from infrequent military and material support, and each battle resulted in heinous damages from both sides. Operation 终 (dān) saw rapid breakthroughs along the frontline, with CRA and ATXR forces capturing the cities of Kurobi, Monanga, and Hamju. Exhausted, the Xiangzhouans suffered a series of catastrophic defeats. CRA, ATXR, and Mudanqiharian forces closed in on Quanhua.
In the end, Quanhua was taken after a month of resistance, with the raising of the Chani flag by CRA units over the Assembly Office symbolizing its end. Non-occupied lands of Xiangzhou were quick to fall into anarchy following the news of Quanhua's capture. In its aftermath, remaining nations aligned themselves with the Chanis, hoping that a peaceful approach would grant greater autonomy to their territories. Victory would be proclaimed on May 18, 1969, accompanied by a parade that would demonstrate the CRA's massive military prowess. The war was not kind to Level 107. The cities were devastated; Quanhua was in ruins, and so were many others. Thousands died, and the animosity rested on the minds of many more, yet exhaustion from years of conflict impeded any immediate action. The Chan Shonun annexed its defeated rivals while leaving those that willingly surrendered or were aligned with them as vassal states. Territorial reforms, especially regarding Xiangzhou, split the province in four with the intention of utilizing preexisting sentiments of regional nationalism to stop the formation of a powerful, central nationalist identity. Avedai Fiao largely addressed the issue of the economy through the financial investment in conglomerates, enforced by political repression. Although its success is debated, it laid out the foundation for the future rise of megacorporations. She promoted the ideals of Chan Nationalism in annexed territories and enforced oppressive policies aimed at controlling the populace. The Chan's authoritarianism fostered a vibrant underground and rebel culture, although it was also fueled by animosity towards the Chanis and democratic idealism. The forms of resistance utilized by rebels varied by group, from countercultural movements to political assassinations or organized protests. {{Blurimage|caption=A rebel equipped with an Arisan-supplied Avtomat Kalashnikova-47 during the 1971 students uprising in Yaowon.|file=The man in the mask.png}} In the early 1970s, the Chan Shonun began investing a greater amount of resources into the military following violent protests in Yeowon and Ji, while additional social control measures, such as extending censorship and keeping tabs and records on a large segment of the population, were implemented. With concerns of a shrinking labor force increasing due to the effects of the war, Avedai Fiao enacted an aggressive natalist policy, which included outlawing abortion and contraception and routine pregnancy tests for women. These pro-natalist measures had some degree of success, as a baby boom resulted during the late 70s. By 1974, the elite guard, the CPRG, who had become a de facto secret police, had become so omnipresent that it made the Chan Shonun an exceptionally brutal police state. Free speech was limited, and opinions that did not favor the All-Chani Party (ACP) were forbidden. Avedai waged an "internal war" intended to purge the nation's underground society; however, it heavily interfered with the regular citizen's life. Civilians angered by the oppressive regime congregated into riots and civic disobedience, sweeping the Chan Shonun and its satellite states. Fighting between protesters and the CPRG became increasingly agitated, while brief clashes in satellite states transformed into uprisings that reinstated or placed new governments in positions of power. Amidst the chaos, Avedai Fiao publicly addressed the nation in the Central Party Structure (CPS) with the intent to garner up her supporters and launch a campaign against "the devils tainted by humanity." However, rather than drawing in her supporters, the majority of attendees were opponents and insurrectionists, who openly booed and jeered Avedai as she spoke. She was forced to call in the CPRG, police, and part of the armed forces to suppress the crowd of her "supporters." Arisa, who held sympathies for the protesters, actively supported the rebels with weaponry and intelligence; before Avedai Fiao gave the green light for the CPRG and police to fire upon the crowd, an Arisan sniper shot and killed her. The immediate aftermath was chaotic, erupting into a vicious battle that left around 130 people dead. News of the assassination spread mainly through word of mouth, as state-sponsored media refused to report the incident. Meanwhile, as indicated by the line of succession, Deputy Supreme Dongmei Chen was supposed to assume power; however, a violent power struggle for the position of Supreme Leader quickly unfolded among the Supreme Council. The Chan Shonun erupted into a brutal civil war on April 19, 1974, with one side consisting of a unified Republican and Democratic faction largely concentrated in Xiangzhou and liberated vassal states, supported by Aurielle, while the opposing loyalist faction was fractured among the leaders Xian Xiuying, Lu Nikitin, Shen Tohyon, and Dongmei Chen, as well as the Ximaoji Realm. Immediately following the breakout of the civil war, Nikitin's clique directed advances against Republican forces in the Guxang, while the ATXR confronted Xiangzhouan insurgents in the east. Several local loyalist uprisings sprang up in Mudanqihar and Cheonhwa, but most were quickly suppressed by rebel forces, and surviving pockets were rendered mostly ineffective in breaking through or seriously threatening Republican positions. In May, forces from Shizi, Gonguo, Neng, and Zhonping breached loyalist lines and captured the Mizhian border settlements of Honshiri and Khulig, while supplying a Mizhian insurrection in the Yakebi Strip. In the beginning of the war, the loyalist cliques each acted as independent entities in agreement with each other to not attack one another; however, as the civil war progressed, they were forced to collaborate. Beginning in July, Dongmei Chen and Shen Tohyon met to reach a temporary cooperation treaty, driven by their mutual enemy and hastened by direct Auriellen involvement in August. From the alliance came forth a large-scale offensive on loyalist territory, in tandem with the ATXR, to reach Ximaoji lines and create a defensive perimeter there. Despite their initial breakthroughs, Dongmei and Shen relied on the resource-intensive strategies from the Xiangzhouan Campaign era, which they were unable to effectively implement. From August to November, Republican forces launched offensives into loyalist-controlled areas. By December 2nd, Democratic insurgents had crossed deep into the Sozin area and were moving to attack ATXR forces. Simountaneously, they also began counterattacks on the southeast, north, and east, and began supplying the slave revolt in Hoeryong. {{Blurimage|caption=Democratic troops flanking loyalist forces during the Spring Offensive.|file=Rocky goons.png|align=left}} 1975 included the three most significant republican military campaign of the civil war; the Xianzihua Campaign, Tongramay Campaign, and Jyusho Campaign. The Xianzihuan Campaign was launched on 8 February 1975 and led by Commander Liu Ch'ol-Su of the Xiangzhouan contingent with the primary goal of recapturing the Shozin. Spearheaded by armored troops and supported by airmobile and artillery assets, Republican assets decisively cut through loyalist defenses, splitting them in two—one of whom became encircled in May. The Xiangzhouan forces, most of whom were veterans, angered by the destruction brought by Chani rule, massacred loyalist defenses in the biggest single act of mutinous violence in the civil war. All involved members were acquitted of their crimes during a post-war ruling. Nonetheless, the campaign came to an end with the capture of Huenang following its siege. The Tongramay Campaign of mid-1975 secured Yeowon and Mizhi. The Haigen Rebellion in Yeowon signalled the beginning of the Tongramay Campaign on June 17th. Offensive actions came under the jurisdiction of Zhonping and Shenzei commanders with direct Auriellen assistance, who commanded forces to break through defenses in Pinxing and meet with Haigen insurrectionists. A large number of loyalist forces deserted or defected as Republican forces approached, and many cities were liberated with little fighting. The Jyusho Campaign of early 1975 to mid-1976 drove a bulk of Democratic forces into the Ximaoji Realm to then attack the Jyusho in a historic flanking maneuver. Often characterized as the most ambitious and bloodiest campaign of the war, both the Republicans and Loyalists suffered heavy casualties during its length. Aurielle supported and sent troops to fight in Nayeong, Henghou, and along the Kenjaban-Dongsan perimeter. By early 1976, Ximaoji had become split, with the eastern side facing an encirclement by Democratic forces, and Jyusho was within the reach of opposing forces. The campaign closed with the capture of Jyusho and the defeat of encircled Ximaoji forces in the east, whose defeat ended their ability to be an effective large-scale fighting force. In August 1976, the Tongramay, Jyusho, and Xianzihuan campaigns ended in decisive Democratic victories. Loyalist forces were forced to pockets of strongholds in Ji, Guxang, Tawagon, and core-Chan Shonun territory. The front had become static as a brutal winter swept Level 107, temporarily placing operations hold; however, in March 1977, the Democratic front launched the final general attack on the loyalist defenders in the Shozuwang Campaign, culminating in the capture of the Chan Shonun's capital and the complete military and governmental collapse of the state. The Siege of Shozuwang is considered by Cascadan historiographers as the most costly battle of the civil war in terms of casualty, destruction, and intensity of fighting. Nearly the entire city of Shozuwang was destroyed by the end of the siege. Beginning in the early mornings of March 12th, with the support of almost the entire artillery force of the Republicans that continuously bombarded Shozuwang, Combined Arms assets punctured a hole in the walls of the city's defenses and began engaging in street fighting with the defenders. By the next day, Republican forces raised the Xiangzhouan flag over the CPS, and in the late night of the same day, the city defenders had been completely annihilated. During the last moments before the fall of the city, senior Loyalist military and government officials carried out a mass suicide, including Xian Xiuying, Lu Nikitin, Dongmei Chen, and Miluo Xinhuilang. Shen Tohyon was captured by Chan democratic resistance forces and summarily executed by firing squad in the Chani Dependency of Gonguo's Governor Palace. ===Zaamtāon Period=== Following the collapse of the Chan Shonun, the Republican front disbanded, resulting in the formation of the 22 states that existed prior to the war. Despite its dissolution, the post-war sphere favored level-wide cooperation, especially when it came to reconstruction and collaboration, which promoted the rise of Pan-Zuhorism and advocates of the creation of a single, unified Cascadan state. Support of Cascadan unionism was exacerbated following the Chani insurgency in Guijing, as it was used by unionists to promote the movement and stop the rise of any Chani uprisings that may plunge Level 107 into another war. The Tawagonian politician Sozhen Gong became a prominent Unionist leader who led the Ku Committee that lobbied the Auriellen Congress to support the creation of an independent Cascada. Gong initially faced resistance from nationalist leaders, who preferred split, independent states; however, they managed to reach a compromise, which was delivered at the Jiangxiang Declaration on 20 July 1979. The topic of unification became largely dormant as other important topics, such as reconstruction, superseded it. {{Blurimage|caption=An example of Urban Harmonization in the Spring.|file=Urban jungle.png}} Between 1980 and 1982, the Cascadan states witnessed the emergence of various youth cultural movements, largely inspired by the 1970s underground rebel culture and Auriellen cultural imports. A style emerged from the post-war landscape, focused on buildings that were damaged yet had been uninhabited. Urban Harmonization, as it became known, was characterized by the merging of the urban structures within the level and nature. The style was made from the concepts of naturalia and "green oases" and birthed the seasonal green markets and Zāon greenhouse. In late 1982, progress on Cascadan unification was rekindled after the Tǒngjat Institute reported over a thousand unsubstantiated cases of apparent Chani actions that would be allegedly performed over the years. These actions were said to trigger a rapid decay of social and governmental order, which could be used to establish a new Chani order. In September 1983, Aurielle stipulated the Yanobe Unification Summit, joined by the leaders of the 22 nations. The negotiations would end with the acceptance of the terms and the proclamation of the Duchy of Cascada. ===Duchy of Cascada=== The Duchy of Cascada began as a fragile political experiment. Despite its support by a good portion of the populace, the Duchy, to no one's surprise, was a deeply unpopular state among nationalists and exceptionalists and was a neutral matter among many more. Although the Cascadists and Nationalists had firmly established opinions regarding their respective groups, the neutral crowd continued to be a significant point of contention and a catalyst for conflict between the two sides. Both factions raced to attract as many supporters as possible to undermine the other side's legitimacy and voter base. Beginning with Kurizhou Zeixeng's government until Dei Huitynl's in 1993, the government was enthralled in a long period of political, economic, and general reform, attributable to the Cascadist-Nationalist Struggle. Zeixeng's government focused greatly on de-Chanification and the consolidation of the Duchy of Cascada as a permanent state. The new government drafted an improved constitution where they formalized every citizen's equality and civil rights and also embarked on a nationwide metropolitan railway system project. Zeixeng faced criticism due to a lack of recompensation towards those affected by the Chan Shonun, his administration's lack of clear economic policy, and shallow promises in education and healthcare. However, those issues remained overshadowed by the 1985 Tsunesaburo Nou Tie Scandal. In March 1985, Tsunesaburo Nou Tie published an analysis of the Cascadan military and had concluded that there were several weaknesses in its structure and training. A week after the publication, the Tsunesaburo Nou Tie article on its analysis had been recalled, and the newspaper's office was raided by police, and quantities of documents were seized due to reasons regarding national security. Zeixeng and Defense Minister Myong Ye-Soung proclaimed that the article was tantamount to treason and would be exploited by nationalists and Chanis to threaten public security and internally damage the Duchy. Public outcry over the blatant violation of freedom of speech and press roared, which led to the resignation of the Defense Minister and the Minister of Internal Affairs, but by then Nationalist leaders had already organized demonstrations that advocated for free speech. A movement known as the "Voice of the Free People" mounted in the aftermath of the Tsunesaburo Nou Tie scandal and the Defense Minister's resignation. They swore to uphold the values of free speech, deemed to be threatened by the sitting government, but were primarily used by nationalists to gradually advance anti-Cascadist ideas. Demonstrations led by the VTFP accelerated tensions between them and the government, and while alleviation of tensions was attempted by government authorities, right-wing politicians within the National Assembly blocked efforts and waged a massive campaign against the protesters, who were declared to be just hooligans and thugs. Nationalist or Chani-affiliated protests faced severe repression, but they frequently employed this as an excuse to suppress peaceful demonstrations. Dissatisfaction with the treatment of demonstrators and the lack of success in achieving their aims led to growing militancy among nationalists, Chanis, and unaffiliated demonstrators alike. Internally, senior members of the military were growing increasingly frustrated over the lack of military funding and expanding factionalism within the army. General Handa Koto led the March 17th plot against Zeixeng's government in 1986, whereon they successfully assassinated the Prime Minister but ultimately failed to instate a military dictatorship as Koto had wished. Despite his subsequent arrest and execution and the inauguration of Kai Zheng as the new prime minister, the assassination of Zeixeng instigated a period of political turmoil. Disgruntled students with allegiance to the VTFP set fire to an apartment block in May 1986, setting off a massive split within the movement that created various separate factions, ranging from liberals to hard-line communists, anarchists, and nationalists. The most notorious of these groups was the National Protection Army, which began by making bank raids to finance their activities and eventually moved to a campaign of indiscriminate bombings designed to create panic, discredit the central government, and provoke a collapse of the Cascadan system. The NPA's action, especially the Zhouliang Bank bombing, started the Hot Winter—a period of social and political violence. Amidst the Hot Winter, far-right, neo-Chani groups, politically marginalized since the fall of the Chan Shonun, initiated a campaign of terror with the goal of establishing a successor to the Chan Shonun. {{Blurimage|caption=Cascadan Army troops raiding a suspected NRA facility.|file=Me and gang.png|align=left}} During the Hot Winter, crime surged dramatically. Left-wing and right-wing extremist groups engaged in arson attacks, kidnapping, assassinations, and terrorist attacks. In between the political violence, opportunistic criminals began popping up, taking part in armed robberies, murder, and kidnappings; a majority of criminals that had appeared in the mid-1980s crime wave were nonaffiliated, although some were influenced by extremist groups. Kai Zheng initiated a comprehensive crackdown on crime and extremist groups in Cascada in 1987. He established the State Intelligence Directorate in February of that year and implemented policies to redirect more funding toward the military and police forces. Efforts were further exacerbated following reports of the creation of an underground snuff film industry, used to fund operations, and the boom of group-inspired mass shootings, most significantly marked by the 1988 Nomkh Plaza massacre, known as the deadliest mass shooting in the Backrooms, prompting the creation of the Militarized Special Police Unit (MSPU) and the usage of the Cascadan Army to assist police in crime and anti-group operations. Zheng loosened market restrictions, increased government subsidies, and gave tax breaks to select companies to stimulate economic growth. His government also announced a 3-year Economic Development Plan and sold Dem Spera, as it had little use and was becoming expensive to upkeep, to Aurielle for a significant amount of money. Much of the acquired capital was invested into education to create a skilled workforce and prevention campaigns with the intent of isolating and starving out extremist groups. In late 1988, the Cascadan Social Democratic Party (CSDP) candidate, Fuise Lang, continued to implement economic policies but also put heavy emphasis on public trust, worker rights, and transparency. Fuise Lang reintroduced market restrictions and held off on tax breaks, temporarily slowing down economic growth. Instead, Lang began opening up Cascada to the outside market, entering a trade agreement with [[Architect Industries]] in 1989, which doubled development. In 1990, Aurielle was invaded and occupied in the Lustruger Crescen War, heavily disrupting Cascada's trade, as the nation had been its only ally since the Eigong Period. Zheng's cabinet refused to trade with the [[M.E.G.]] and took a pro-Auriellen stance in the conflict, taking in refugees from the war. Above all, however, was the threat posed by economic recession. Following the news of the fall of Aurielle, the National Assembly passed a bill that opened up Cascada to the outside world, allowing for foreign products and businesses to settle in the nation. Cascada saw rapid export-led economic growth, which further empowered the domestic businesses—many now considered mega corporations. While extremist groups continued to operate well into the late 1980s and into the early 1990s, the upmost point of violence of the Hot Winter had effectively ended. Crime rates began to steadily decline as increased police enforcement arrested and prosecuted notorious killers, robbers, and senior members of extremist groups. The most notable trial during this period was that of Hirano Kaii-Si, the leader of the Red Army. The crisis symbolically concluded with the abolishment of the NPA and the defeat of the Split Order, both in 1993. Prior to elections, Lang controversially defunded the police and army, redirecting them elsewhere. The outbreak of the war and failure by Cascada's government to act on it led to the election of Dei Huitynl, a known militarist and right-wing figure who was a candidate of the National Liberal Movement (NLM). Huitynl embarked on a vast rearmament and modernization program, bolstering the defense industry and, in turn, the economy, which grew at an unparalleled rate as its true potential had been achieved after the threat of terrorism had been diminished. Mega corporations, who came under the name Dāogungsi, fully entrenched their position in the Cascadan economy. Moreover, his rule greatly expanded Cascada's international image and export, alongside strengthening ties with the Auriellen resistance and Geminia. Under Huitlynl's administration, the National Assembly passed the contentious Territorial Expansion Act, kickstarting a period of Cascadan imperialism. Under the legislation, Cascada, with the assistance of Surzhou, established the Fujishiri autonomous region in Level 330.1. The act would not be used again until 1997, when Cascada invaded Greater Devysroy, using the Stilhem Massacre as a pretext for carrying out military action. In its aftermath arose the Autonomous Region of Hannamo and the Cascadan-Suburbian Conflict. Huitlynl was re-elected as prime minister in 1998, although his second term was far less popular, mostly due to his mishandling of the Cascadan position in Level 79. {{Blurimage|caption=The Token Defense System in action.|file=Femtanyl stuff or something.png}} Soon after winning his second term, Huitlynl's government launched an invasion of the separated states of Clavis and Devysroy. The goal was to reinstate Kian Koekemoer and Semir Kastelic as democratic leaders after their ousting in a coup. During this conflict, Hannamoan population centers were shelled by opposing forces, which led to the implementation of the Token Defense System, completed in 2003. Most nations a part of the Coalition of Suburbian Nations (SNC) took an aggresive position, signalled by the "Three Nos" resolution, in the topic of Cascadan territory within Suburbia. Cascada faced attacks by a far-right pan-Suburbian paramilitary, the Cascadis Khofnerye Hunters, joined by the Clavis Liberation Front (CLF) and Army for Devysroyian Liberation (ADL) in Operation Hunting Hour. Cascada waged a counterinsurgency campaign in Clavis and Devysroy but was forced to withdraw since they had no long-term strategy for the region; however, they continued to battle CKH insurgents. In September 2001, Cascada carried out a massive assassination wave against Gierenian and Tolistani leaders. After major protests erupted in Piqieng and Nomkh concerning the Cascadan settlement in Suburbia, the Settler's Act was enacted. This consequential amendment reserved future expansions for unsettled areas in an effort to address the concerns of demonstrators. In 2002, Cascada took an active role in the Korthie Casiagen War, with their first action being the invasion of M.E.G. territory in Level 107. The Joint Defense Chief Office expedited the creation of the Cascadan Expeditionary Force (CEF), the primary fighting force assigned to the Korthie Casiagen War. Additionally, in the same year, Cascada sent more forces to Hannamo as the Cascadan Armed Forces (CAF) and Tolistani Land Forces skirmished on the border, raising tensions. Huitlynl was involved in a scandal following the reemergence of medical records, which indicated that he had been assigned female at birth and had received surgery to transition later down the line. This infuriated conservative and religious members, who deemed it unnatural and unholy, while also stimulating false rhetoric regarding transgenderism and testosterone. The government issued a statement that underlined that no criminal investigation or action would be undertaken, as transitioning was not illegal within Cascada. Despite the passing of the Settler's Act, Cascada shelled Tolistani positions in 2003, followed by an invasion of South Tolistan. An SNC resolution urged Cascada to immediately withdraw forces from Southern Tolistan, but they did not comply with it and went on to occupy a borderland buffer zone, run by the Free Tolistani Republic, a Cascadan proxy. Before the elections, Huitlynl implemented anti-corruption reforms and initiated an extensive plan for internet accessibility and the modernization of colonial territory. {{Blurimage|caption=Foreign investors observing the stock market during the 2004 Cascadan financial crisis.|file=Yasuda.png|align=left|size=400px}} Hwo So-Yung of the Democratic Civic Platform (DCP) won the 2013 election. So-Yung continued and finalized Huitlynl's anti-corruption reforms, internet, and modernization plans but took a different route in regards to foreign policy, especially concerning Suburbia, following the effort of Suburbian-Cascadan normalization. In spite of plans, So-Yung was almost immediately met with news of hostilities in Suburbia. The National Resistance Movement (NRM), an anti-Cascadan organization, overtook the Popular Clavian Liberation Front in support within Clavis. Cascada labelled the group as hostile and supported the PCLF, however So-Yung had no plans to outright intervene in the Clavian politics. Weaknesses in the economy and excessive leverage led to the 2004 Cascadan financial crisis, which continued until early 2006. So-Yung legislated monetary and labor reforms to address the crisis while also asking allies for assistance, primarily through monetary programs. Cascada entered agreements and bolstered relations with Anthra, both as a part of normalization efforts and for assistance in the financial crisis, although their support was limited by the SNC. In 2005, the NRM bombardment of Hannamo precipitated an invasion of Clavis by Cascada and Unu Citedum. In 2006, Anthra reconsidered its stance on the Cascadan situation and had effectively terminated its limitations on establishing relations and cooperating with the Duchy to pursue peace with Cascada under the Dungtouyun Accords. Peace talks extended into early 2009, when the Guangmen Peace Agreement was finally reached and ratified. This agreement resulted in the Anthran recognition of Hannamo as part of Cascadan territory, established mutual trade agreements, and included guarantees that Cascada would refrain from attacking Anthra. Additionally, it stipulated that Anthra could not allow SNC forces to traverse their territories if they intended to attack Cascada or support aggressors. After the signing of the Guangmen Peace Agreement, the CKH reactivated, although in a much weaker form, and collaborated with the Suburbian Interest Defense League to murder and kidnap Cascadan nationals in Hannamo. In 2008, Prime Minister Trang Hong-Yang oversaw the invasion of Tolistan to destroy SIDL and CKH targets. In what was a response to Devysroyian bombing attacks, Cascada invaded east Devysroy to destroy artillery targets and implanted a collaborationist rebel group in 2010. From 2011 to 2012, Cascadan agents and artillery struck CHF military facilities to dwindle their capabilities. In 2012, Cascada responded to Clavian artillery strikes by invading once again. During the late stages of the Fokunuja Offensive during the Korthie Casiagen War, CEF troops were deployed to [[Level 797]]. Information about the level, along with several pictures, was sent back to the central government. They determined that the environment was suitable for settlement and expressed interest in acquiring and utilizing the level's geothermal waters for their healing properties. The Rankita autonomous region would be established in June 2013. On 1 March 2014, the day of the Jotean holiday of Réi Nou Kǎr, the Tolistani, CHF, and Clavian armies launched a surprise attack against Cascadan forces in Hannamo, opening up the first phase of the SNC-Cascadan War. CHF and Tolistani forces shelled Cascadan targets in company with rocket attacks staged by Clavis, followed by a ground invasion of Hannamo through the Heitenasian Strip and less defended FTR-controlled areas, for CHF and Tolistan, and the south and east for Clavis.
