Fighting broke out in the Aramon and Rupes, and the Suburbian military coalition was joined by the CKH and SIDL, who concentrated in the Rupes. Advances were halted by the 8th, and the Cascadans managed to push back the coalition soon afterwards. {{Blurimage|caption=Cascadan artillery attacks site of Clavis rocket launch in the beginning of the Cascadan-SNC War.|file=Boom shakalaka.png}} Prime Minister Yan Toshi stated that they would not accept an armistice or ceasefire to end the conflict, and called the attack cowardly. Cascada called upon Unu Citedum to invade Clavis, offering a significant amount of territory, and they offered territorial concessions to the 6th Army if they were to attack Tolistan from the north. Both accepted, and Cascada begun Operation Of Their Own Accord, tasked with the total defeat of Tolistan and Clavis. The attacks garnered the attention of the SNC, who deemed it as a threat to the integrity of a Suburbian nation and subsequently passed a resolution intervening in the conflict in order to restore peace and security; Aurielle intervened in the side of the Duchy following the resolution's passing. By then, however, Cascada had pushed Tolistan to the brink of total occupation, while also capturing CHF border towns. Clavis came under total occupation soon after Aurielle intervened. The SNC launched an offensive that reestablished northeast Tolistani territory before Auriellen bombardment and counteroffensive operations halted coalition advancements. Additional forces were sent to Hannamo in order to reinforce the northwest perimeter, which managed to break the SNC offensive's momentum and allowed the development of a massive encirclement in the CHF. After a strategic withdrawal in Teurkhvost tricked the SNC into believing that the area was collapsing, Cascadan, Auriellen, and FTR forces in Falmaer and Tushma rapidly advanced to meet up with forces in Kenhurt, encircling a large portion of the northwestern SNC group in Haal. Entrapped forces were subject to constant harassment by Cascadan forces and their defeat was imminent, while in the northeast the 6th Army, Aurielle, and Unu Citedum began scoring victories. Following a ceasefire in Haal, an armistice, per Tehyovikwozhu's Resolution 251 proposal, was reached, and separate disengagement talks took place between Cascada, Kastenie, Dzofar, and Kada. Negotiations culminated in an armistice that antecdeted the Kankaanrainen Treaties, marking the end of the Cascadan-SNC War on July 12th. Cascada claimed victory and held most of the demand-making capabilities in the treaties: the Kankaanrainen Treaties marked the demilitarization of Clavis and Tolistan, established a demilitarized zone between the 6th Army and Tolistan, promoted peace with Cascada by SNC members, annexed areas of Clavis to Cascada and Unu Citedum and areas of northern Tolistan to the 6th Army, and ended the support of anti-Cascadan militias in exchange for the bilateral payment of reparations, the end of the military occupation of Clavis, Tolistan, and areas of CHF, and the exchange of prisoners of war. Cascada circumvented opposition towards the creation of a liberal democracy in South Tolistan by demilitarizing the Surem-Norshin Line, which led to an FTR-dominated south. Kastenie became the second Suburbian country to normalize relations with Cascada after they signed the Jinzihuan Agreement, marking the beginning of a trend of normalization. Originating from the Jinzihuan Agreement came the normalization of relations with Mechta, Albazine, and Couvea; relations with Dzofar and Kada improved. With peace established in Suburbia, Cascada refocused more on the Korthie Casiagen War. Yan Toshi initially planned for the complete withdrawal of the CEF, but Auriellen backing in the SNC-Cascadan War made the decision feel disgraceful. Toshi opted for a partial withdrawal while filling in the spots with new recruits, combined with seasoned volunteers from the SNC-Cascadan War. Mental health became a heavily debated topic from 2015 onwards as studies modeling trauma exposure and assessing mental health outcomes estimated a sharp rise in the probability of developing PTSD, especially among returning veterans. The National Institute of Mental Health was created to address the rise of PTSD, anxiety, and depression, primarily using the Ncipetherazi model as inspiration. Cascada established its last settlement, the Watanokawa autonomous region within [[Level 264]], in 2016 after the Shānseuk Campaign drove CEF soldiers to The Ground layer in the level. Toshi began the process of withdrawing from the Korthie Casiagen War and officially finalized CEF missions in 2018 but continued to support Aurielle through other means, as the government felt as if Aurielle did not need to be supported militarily any longer. In October 2018, an anonymous whistleblower leaked files regarding accounts of fraud and sexual abuse committed by various CEOs of megacorporations to Cascadan journalists. The Hérsounū Leaks pitted the government against the influential Dāogungsi and was the main reason for the passing of the Observer's Act. Of the nine involved Dāogungsi CEOs, seven were criminally prosecuted, while the remaining two were acquitted of most crimes due to insufficient evidence and instead charged with fines for unrelated crimes. Liberal Democratic Party of Cascada (LDPC) candidate Pin Yaoxi won a close election over United Front candidate Lee Jae-kaemon by the narrowest margin in the history of the Duchy. Yaoxi initiated a vast crackdown on corruption and crime while also preparing Cascada and its territories for an extensive infrastructure and electrical modernization plan. Cascada became a founding member of the Volucris Strategic Economic and Security Accord (VSESA) in 2019, although it limited itself to the alliance's Common Political Chamber, and, per VSESA Central Economic Union regulations, Cascada formally adopted the Nolic as its secondary currency. Following months of Anti-Entity Group cross-border raids, A-2 declared war on Cascada on April 17th, 2020, initiating the [[Cascadan Conflict]]. Soon after A-2's declaration of war, AEG forces fully entered Cascada and launched offensive operations against defensive lines. The AEG's momentum briefly faltered in Malsai, exposing a critical weakness when it came to anti-guerrilla operations, which was heavily taken advantage of. The AEG sent reinforcements to reinforce a western offensive, but they were repelled by Cascadan forces. After a firefight between K4 and stationed Auriellen Ground Forces troops stationed in the Alastor Ground Forces Garrison, Aurielle partially intervened on the side of Cascada. {{Blurimage |file=Cascadan tanks doing cascadan tanks things.png |caption=Cascadan YQ-9 Lǎong tanks during the Cascadan Conflict.}} The AEG contracted [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/I.R.I.S. I.R.I.S] to carry out large-scale cyberattacks across Cascadan military facilities, which infected Auriellen devices and brought upon full Auriellen intervention, accompanied by Antlia. Cascada and its allies pushed back AEG forces to three cities, Jeonsan, Sunai, and Huidong, where the AEG and K4 made a last stand. Enemy units began withdrawing as the cities began collapsing. In its aftermath, the Salvemini Security Treaty was signed, and Cascada joined VSESA's Mutual Defense Treaty. A-1 and other Partialist members assumed responsibility in reaching a positive agreement, which came in with the Daveron Agreement. From the Daveron Agreement, an AEG embassy was established in Cascada, and the AEG and Cascada joined the Level 107 Intergovernmental Mutual Cooperation Treaty and the Asuken Guarantee. Amidst subsequent efforts to rebuild, lack of government "retribution" against humanity's actions galvanized sects of Cascadans into perpetrating acts of sectarian violence. What followed was the creation of the extremist Cascadan Black League (KHL), who joined with the Poppies, a Saksukeem militia, and the Masked Maidens, a semi-anarchist anti-corruption group, and began an offensive against Auriellen and Cascadan military targets, marking the beginning of the [[Cascadan Emergency]]. Cascada activated VSESA Mutual Defense Treaty's clause of mutual defense, putting the 23-nation alliance at war against the KHL and its allies. The Cascadan Security Assistance Force, the security mission of the alliance, was joined by the AEG following the Waypoint Emissary hostage crisis in 2022. The Eternal Flame joined the KHL's fight due to their similar stance on humanity. M.E.G., Z.E.T.A., and the United Glowfolk Alliance allegedly supported the KHL, with ZETA having directly supplied and taught the Black League's development of a bioweapon based on the GIG virus. The Masked Maidens signed an armistice with the coalition, eventually surrendering and joining it as a result of the Huijin Agreement in 2023.
In February 2024, an Auriellen-Cascadan covert operation captured KHL leader Yu Fang. The Cascadan armed forces, supported by CSAF, as well as allied anti-KHL militias, then initiated a counteroffensive to retake and liberate KHL and Poppies-held territory. In April 2024, when both the Poppies and KHL had lost all their territory, the government declared victory. Diplomatic relations between the UGA and Cascada were dissolved due to alleged support of the KHL by their government. The UGA embassy in Cascada was stormed by protesters during an event known as the Shantou Struggle. This incident coincided with the evacuation of Cascadan diplomats from their embassy in UGA due to concerns about potential reprisals. Cascada also joined the international War against Zeta's Operation Evolutionary Resolve. ==Politics== Cascada is a parliamentary republic and constitutional monarchy in which the power of the duke (Wòng) is limited to a ceremonial role. Executive power is instead wielded by the prime minister and the Cabinet, whose sovereignty is vested in the Cascadan people. Following the formation of the Duchy of Cascada, Zeixeng's sweeping amendments to the constitution restructured the state's government branches while maintaining a balance between its framework as a unitary state and greater decentralization to regional governments. Xiong Hanying is the duke of Cascada, having succeeded his father, Chanyu Hanying, upon his accession to the throne in 2021. {{Blurimage |file=National assembly building.png |caption=Empty insides of the National Assembly Building.}} Cascada's legislative body is the National Assembly, a bicameral parliament that is responsible for amending the constitution, inaugurating and impeaching the president, and formalizing state policies. The National Assembly consists of the lower House of Representatives, consisting of 580 members, and the upper Palace of Councillors, which has 152 members who serve six-year terms. Cascada's judicial system is exercised by the supreme courts and high courts. The highest judicial body is the Supreme Court of Cascada, possessing 15 judges. The Supreme Court of Cascada interprets the constitution and other laws and decrees, judges administrative suits, disciplines public functionaries, and handles final appeals and case reviews. The Constitutional Court adjudicates constitutional and political matters, regulates the activities of political parties, and arbitrates disputes between the central and regional governments. Additionally, the Judicial Commission monitors judicial performance, and works to uphold the integrity and independence of the judiciary. ===Political Parties=== Electoral politics in Cascada have been characterized by a competitive multi-party system since 1995, wherein no single political party has secured an outright majority of seats in the legislative elections. Despite the lack of a hegemonic political party, five parties remain the proportionally dominant ones within the state, being the Cascadan Social Democratic Party, National Liberal Movement, Democratic Civic Platform, Liberal Democratic Party of Cascada, and Justice Path Party. {| class="article-table" | colspan="2" |'''Name''' |'''Abbr.''' |'''Leader(s)''' |'''Ideology''' |'''Political position''' |- | |Cascadan Social Democratic Party |CSDP |Sun Yijun, Yesuongke Hai |Social democracy |Center-left |- | |National Liberal Party |NLP |Kan Jian-Yeol |National conservatism Right-wing Populism |Far-right |- | |Democratic Civic Platform |DCP |Ejiri Maeko |Conservatism Regionalism |Right-wing |- | |Liberal Democratic Party of Cascada |LDPC |Lian Qigang |Liberalism Progressivism
Liberal Feminism |Center-left |- | |Justice Path Party |JPP |Gao Ruogang |Cascadan Nationalism Liberal Conservatism
National Liberalism |Center-right |} ===Administrative divisions=== Cascada is constitutionally a unitary state divided into 22 provinces, overseen by an elected governor and legislature, and 43 administrative areas, led by administrators, as well as 7 autonomous regions. The provinces, administrative states, and two (each with a designated minority group) of the seven autonomous regions make up what is collectively referred to as "mainland Cascada," all being within Level 107. The remaining autonomous regions of Tabù, Fujishiri, Hannamo, Rankita, and Watanokawa are referred to as "Greater Cascadan territories." ==Military== {{Blurimage |file=Spiritually israeli.png |caption=YQ-9 Lǎong battle tanks of the Cascadan Army.}} The Cascadan Armed Forces is the unified military force of the country and is headed by its Joint Defense Chief Office, subordinate to the Minister of Defense. The Cascadan Armed Forces consists of the army, air force, and gendarmerie. The army and air force of the armed forces were founded during the Yanobe Unification Summit by consolidating each state's military into one; the gendarmerie's creation did not occur until the Hot Winter. The CAF has been involved in several major wars and border conflicts, making it one of the most battle-trained armed forces in the Backrooms. Most serving Cascadans join at 19 to 21. Men serve two years and ten months, and women serve two years and two months before being able to join the reserve forces. Reservists usually do up to several weeks of reserve duty every year until their forties; however, most women are exempted from auxiliary duty. Pre-Cascadan Suburbian residents in Hannamo, along with humans and lawful permanent immigrants, are eligible to enlist in the military. The military relies heavily on high-tech weapons systems designed and manufactured in Cascada as well as some foreign, mainly Auriellen, imports. Cascada's Token Defense System air defense system gained Backrooms-wide acclaim after intercepting hundreds of artillery and rockets fired by Suburbian militaries. Since then, Cascada has retained one of the most esteemed defense industries, known for weapon accessories, military vehicles, small arms, and helicopters. Cascada is among the Backrooms' largest conventional arms exporters after the superpowers such as the Republic of Aurielle, M.E.G., and A.E.G. The most popular types of weaponry bought from Cascada are firearms, attachments, air defense systems, armored personnel carriers, and infantry fighting vehicles. Cascadan arms were exported to over 45 countries; the most supplies and the most significant market remain Cascadan allies in VSESA, which accounted for over 60 percent of deliveries. Helicopters and tanks are the two products that remain exclusive to specific allies and are not widely available for the wider market due to strategic concerns. Military expenditure heavily increased during the Cascadan-Suburbian Conflict and has constituted a considerable portion of the country's gross domestic product. Since its inception, the Republic of Aurielle has been a particularly notable contributor of military aid. Cascada's inception as a user, and later on master, of military hardware began with the introduction of firearms by Aurielle in the Persan-Zuhoru trade; Aurielle continued to provide updated firearms throughout the year up until information over the acquisition of firearms was given by Aurielle in the late 1950s. By the 1960s a large firm systematically gathered and executed [[Entity 25|Counter-Entities]] for its resources and weapons and was made by various different groups within Level 107, providing them with firearms. The investigation regarding Frontrooms and military systems led to the creation of the APC and APFV projects. Helicopters and their military equipment further assisted in the topic of hardware gathering. Modernization of military equipment became a necessity for the Chan Shonun during its campaigns, which were further intensified following its victory in the war that exceeded industrialization to maintain the dictatorship. Over the years, expertise in military technologies became a significant focus of research and development, facilitating the growth of its defense industry. This focus became required as Cascada became entangled in various conflicts. ==Economy== Cascada is a highly industrialized mixed economy partially reliant on international trade, especially Auriellen, with a total GDP per capita of 937 金. Cascada possesses an enormous fishing industry and is the largest exporter of Mawfish in the Backrooms. Its economy has long been dominated by the fishing industry, the fur trade, both markets, which are nationalized, and manufacturing. The hydrocarbon industry has emerged as a developing sector within the Duchy since the discovery of crude oil deposits on the outskirts of Level 107 during the 2000s. Additionally, web-focused development is also gaining traction as an emerging economic area. Cascada's currency is the Zhou (金), introduced in Shizi during the 1930s, reintroduced following the collapse of the Chan Shonun, and made the official currency of Cascada due to its stability in the Yanobe Unification Summit. Zheng's massive investment into education elevated the nation to a major powerhouse. Cascada's national economy benefits from a highly skilled workforce and is among the most educated countries in the Backrooms. Fuise Lang's strong focus on public trust and initiatives aimed at enhancing transparency has primarily benefited the fur trade, which depends on educating the public about donating their surplus fur to the government. This approach has facilitated the establishment of charities dedicated to funding specific causes. Cascada developed a free-market system similar to Ncipetheraz's Vizhuism (Zhùjì) wherein corporations can only register for one specific market and need to create mostly independent sister companies to expand into other markets, and companies outside of Cascada are required to create a Cascadan branch to operate within it, with the exception of some Auriellen companies. Cases of corruption have happened due to Auriellen companies leveraging their influence over Cascada's industry to pass bills that benefit them, although such incidents remain low. {{Blurimage |file=Nook the goat v2.png |caption=Drawing of Cascadans in Dúng-Dǎing market. |width=320px}} During the unification of Cascada, the vastly different economic state of the attending states became a matter of concern. The issue was solved by allowing the government to control prices, regardless of the involvement of supply and demand, to standardize prices among the territories of the Duchy. The Dāogungsi remain influential within Cascada's economy and have historically profited from war. Lifetime employment and seniority-based career advancement are common in the Cascadan work environment, which, despite advancements, continues to be notoriously toxic and severely lacking in workers' rights. Cascada ranks highly for competitiveness and economic freedom. Furthermore, Cascada was one of the few groups that was able to avoid a recession during [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]. Cascada's economic growth rate reached 3.2% in 2018, a sharp recovery from economic growth rates of 1.3% in 2016. The unemployment rate also remained low in 2016 at 3.6%, with employment doubling as the electric industry witnessed a historic uptick in employment from government advertisements to solve the issue of the blackout in mainland Cascada and Hannamo. ==Demographics== Cascada has the largest Cascadan species population and is the only country in which Cascadans make up more than 2% of the total national population. As of April 2025, the population of the Duchy was an estimated 288,503, of which 31,583 are humans, most coming from Hannamo. It is the most populous nation in the Backrooms, passing New Britain by a margin of 25,000. The number of humans living in Cascada that did not live in Hannamo prior to its annexation in 1997 is 17,134. 21% of them consist of wanderers with no previous background in any other groups (labelled as [[Frontrooms]]-originated), while the remaining are from Aurielle, Tolistan, Unu Citedum, Branuba, and other groups. A majority of Cascadans live in urban areas. The capital city, Piqieng, is the largest city. It is part of the Xǖn Piqieng Area, the biggest metropolitan area in the Backrooms. Cascada is a biologically and ethnically homogeneous society. Outside of humans, minority ethnic groups in the country include the Saksukeem and Guiguzie people. Minority species groups in the country are a miniscule and socially outcast [[Glowfolk]] population, and Furaochàle, divided between those of Hannamonian and Auriellen descent. Cascada's fertility rate in its mainland territory is 5.3, while its greater territory has lower fertility rates. Hannamo stands at some 3.8, while the others are within the range of 2 to 3.1. Tabù is not accounted for due to giving birth being technically illegal there. The nation has a median age of 23. ===Religion=== Cascada is defined as a secular nation within its constitution, which also guarantees full religious freedom. According to the 2024 census, 44.3% of Cascada's population practices Joteo, 25.2% are Christians, 19.6% follow Pyunchijoism, and 10.9% adhere to other religions, such as Zhangism, Islam, Paganism, Florism, and Nymphaeaism, or are irreligious. The level of participation in religious ceremonies as a cultural tradition remains high, especially during festivals and occasions such as Réi Nou Kǎr on the 1st of March. {{Blurimage |file=Paths to among us.png |caption=The Reconciliation's Path leading to the Saijogao Temple in Hengzhou.}} The largest religion in Cascada is Joteo and is followed mostly by Cascadans and Furaochàles. Christianity is the second largest religion in Cascada and is also practiced by Cascadans but is mainly followed by Auriellens and other human immigrants. Pyunchijoism is followed by most Saksukeem and Guiguzie people. Florism is predominantly practiced in Hannamo. Joteo has been the predominant religion within Cascada. Joteo originates in the Sou Shogunate and extended to Level 107 through the Long March and the Sou's War in the East. Christianity was introduced in the 15th century by Surzhou missionaries. Christianity found fertile ground in Languo and other states, who, by the latter parts of the 1400s, helped in the expansion of Christianity in Xiangzhou and other states that were religiously neutral or apathetic to it. Christianity as a symbol of industrialization during the Persan discovery of Cascada led to the religion's proliferation during efforts to modernize. The Christian population declined in the advent of the creation of the Chan Shonun, as it became identified with human influence and therefore was subject to harsh religious oppression during the Chani regime. Nonetheless, Christianity has been regrowing since the creation of the Duchy. Pyunchijoism originated in Saksukeem and expanded as Saksukeem conquered the Guiguzien tribes. The religion reached the central territory of Level 107 during the Zhou Dynasty. By the 1940s, 21.1% of the population of Cascada practiced Pyunchijoism; however, due to religious crackdowns by the Chani regime, massacres, and wars, Pyunchijoism lost 3.6% of its followers. Since the overthrow of the Chani regime, Pyunchijoism has continued to recover and is now classified as a cultural relic. Most preachers of Zhangism are of the Furaochàle species, primarily from the Hannamoan community, and Cascada is home to the second largest population of Zhangism in the Backrooms, just behind Surzhou. The spread of Zhangism in Cascada occurred due to migration to the region. The Florist minority within Cascada exists due to the annexation of Hannamo and Tolistani migrants. ===Ethnic groups=== Cascada's population is ethnically diverse despite being a homogeneous country, with a total of around 7 ethnic groups. Cascadans are the largest ethnic and species group, making up 87.24% (Zǚxaujin 79%, Saksukeem 5%, and Guiguzie 2%) of the population, while humans make up 10.71%, dominated by Auriellen mestizos and Suburbian whites. Glowfolk, Furaochàle, and other human ethnic groups constitute the remainder of Cascada's ethnic population. Cascadans (Zǚxaujin, Saksukeem, and Guiguzie) are the largest species and ethnic group in Cascada. Cascadans were derived from humans that arrived in Level 107 approximately 110,000 years ago. They have been the predominant group in the region since then. Zǚxaujins are the largest Cascadan ethnic group, forming around 79% of Cascadans in the region, while the remaining are divided between the ethnic minorities of Saksukeem and Guiguzie. The Saksukeem and Guiguzie people mostly inhabit the outskirts of Level 107. Humans are the second largest species and ethnic group. The first humans settled in Cascada in the late 1920s following the first contact between the Persans and Zuhoru. The human population significantly expanded in the aftermath of its discovery, although they faced extreme hardships under the Chan Shonun. The human population rebounded after the civil war concluded and experienced significant growth following Cascada's invasion of Greater Devysroy. With 19,901 human residents, Hannamo is home to the largest human community in Cascada. The Furaochàle, the result of human and Cascadan interbreeding, were not recorded in Cascada until the 1950s. Their population remained relatively low up until the annexation of Greater Devysroy and the Cascadan settlement in Level 79, which witnessed its abrupt rise. Within Cascada, there exists a minuscule Glowfolk community, dating back to the early 2000s. Minority ethnic groups in Cascada have experienced numerous instances of racial discrimination and political disenfranchisement throughout history, particularly during the ultranationalist Cascadan regime. However, the mistreatment of these groups dates back even further. One of the earliest instances of minority mistreatment was the segregation of the Saksukeem and Guiguzie people under the Zhou Dynasty, stemming from the perception of them as uncivilized people. Under the Saikhet Realm, the period of civilization stipulated by the Zhou Dynasty witnessed brutal human rights violations and the enslavement of the unruly into submission. By the fall of the Zhou Dynasty, the Saksukeem and Guiguzie had "civilized enough" to be seen as equal to others. Discrimination remained relatively low up until the Chani regime. Under the Chani regime, ethnic groups faced a massive genocide initiated by Cascadan ultranationalists, where humans and other groups ended up in concentration camps for labor purposes. Humans were heavily targeted under the Chan Shonun; most ended up being enslaved into labor, few were subject to special treatment due to useful skills, while the useless were killed. ===Languages=== [[Jinyi]] is the official and national language of the Duchy. Jinyi incorporates a significant number of aspects loaned from East Asian languages, most evident in its writing system, Hànzì and Kokuji Kanji. Jinyi also utilizes the Latin alphabet and Roman numerals. Aurielleno has taken a major role in Cascada as a business and international link language, as well as influencing Jinyi, and is a compulsory subject at the junior and senior high school levels. Auriellen Sign Language is the primary sign language used in Cascada and has gained some official recognition, but its usage has been historically hindered by a lack of educational support. Despite this, Cascadan users of the sign language have severely contributed to the expansion of Auriellen Sign Language. Besides Jinyi, the Guigatsem and Roykualtonese, both part of the Xǖn-Yǎfēng language family, are spoken primarily in Khurohawa and Kaedong autonomous region. The number of children learning these languages is dwindling, but local governments have sought to increase awareness of them. Additionally, a number of other languages are taught and used by ethnic minorities, immigrant communities, and a growing number of foreign-language students, primarily Suburbis English. ===Education=== The Ministry of Education oversees the process for the education of children from kindergarten to the third and final year of high school and manages and provides education. Primary and secondary education is compulsory for ages 7 to 16 years old. All basic education is free for every Cascadan citizen. In a 2022 census, the overall literacy rate in Cascada was around 93.3%, with an illiteracy rate of 6.7%, mostly observed in rural and remote areas. The school year is divided into two semesters, with a short break in between, the first going from the beginning of March and ending in mid-June, and the second beginning in late July and ending in mid-September, avoiding the harshest of the winter season. Cascada is among the top-performing in VSESA countries in reading literacy, mathematics, and sciences. The country has one of the Backrooms's highest-educated labor forces. Education is held in high regard within the nation, with most citizens having done or completed tertiary education. Educational success is often tied with a source of honor and pride for families and within Cascadan society at large and is seen as a fundamental necessity to improve one's socioeconomic position within the state. Government spending on education constitutes 5.6% of the GDP. The 3 top-ranked universities in Cascada are the University of Piqiang, Nomkh University, and Xiangzhou Province University. ===Healthcare=== As of 2020, the average life expectancy in Cascada is 71.8 years, with men living an average of 70.8 years and women living an average of 73.1 years. Prior to the unification of Cascada, its life expectancy was 65.8 years, significantly influenced by the conflicts of the 1960s and 1970s. Following the economic boom in the early 1990s, life expectancy recovered and continues to grow. The Ministry of Health administers healthcare in most of the regions, while some, primarily those of autonomous regions, retain their health authorities. Most hospitals in Cascada are public, funded, and owned by the government. Private hospitals and clinics have witnessed a rise since 1991. They are still subject to government regulations and policies. Healthcare expenditure in 2019 accounted for 9.7% of GDP; most healthcare spending comes from public sources. All Cascadan residents' healthcare is covered by national insurance, and it's universal for all residents. The government prepares an annual budget for each subsequent year, which encompasses all expenses related to the healthcare sector in Cascada. Mental health became a matter of concern in 2015 and was officially integrated as part of healthcare in 2018. The National Institute of Mental Health focuses on mental health issues and conducts studies related to psychological illnesses. It also compiles censuses that detail the nation's mental well-being and engages in efforts to enhance cognitive health. Cascada works closely with the [[Backrooms Red Cross]] and the [[Backrooms Health Organisation]] to better the nation's healthcare. ==Culture== Cascadan culture has historically been influenced by different cultures and ideals, most recently Auriellen. In general, the culture of the Duchy is concerned with maintaining face—a person's good reputation and standing in the eyes of others. Familial cohesion, respect for the family and the ancestor, and respect for the elders of an individual are cornerstones of Cascadan culture. Superiors and elders must be treated commendably and are to be seated and served before others. They are addressed using specific honorifics. Furthermore, they determine the start of mealtime and typically hold the highest position in the household social hierarchy, which governs the order of seating, serving, and bathing in households. Collectivist values are enforced in schools and elsewhere. The concept of social accountability and collective responsibility represents a significant obligation and is typically exerted through practices such as tipping and contributing to government charities. Those who don't tip are usually pressured to do so and are considered to have failed a fundamental duty in harmonization, unless in extenuating circumstances. Regulations are implemented by the government to avoid the exploitation of Cascada's society's gratuity by corporations, especially foreign ones. The immense emphasis on education has led to strict parenting that ensures the child's success (Seoìyào parenting) becoming a norm within Cascadan society. Seoìyào parenting rarely escalates to physical punishment, as social accountability serves as a safeguard against it. Parents who commit domestic abuse for the first time are typically sent to reeducation facilities. Additionally, parents have access to consultatories that offer positive parenting classes. Religion is heavily intertwined with the language and daily life within Cascada. Although its materialization varies by religious group, religious expressions similar to English's "Jesus Christ" and "Oh My God" are commonly utilized by the Cascadan populace, and rites of passage and prayers after food and before it are the most prevalent. Furthermore, the modern philosophical teachings of Xǜ Mǎo, popular within Cascada, about peace between balance, inner self, and stability, are influenced by the Zhangist teachings of Chēn Tǎng. Cascada's distinction between the upper and lower classes is quite evident. The upper class in Cascada predominantly receives education from sophisticated private institutions and has the opportunity to learn from a diverse range of tutors who cultivate cultural capital. Unlike other nations, whose upper class enthrall themselves in both lowbrow and highbrow forms of culture, Cascada's upper class is much more explicit in their identification, viewing lowbrow culture as unmistakably uncivilized and only consuming highbrow media. Despite this, the upper class continues to employ a language that prioritizes form over cultural substance. They possess the capability to conduct detached analyses and engage in discussions about technique and historical context, a skill believed to have been inherited from the upper classes of other nations. There exists a divide between the traditional Cascadan culture (Qǖ Xaofé) and the "ostracized" culture (Nou Xaofé). Nou Xaofé is, by elitist standards, a form of lowbrow culture expanding into a diverse range of subcultures, fashions, and lifestyles that exist in direct opposition to Qǖ Xaofé. Rooted in the underground rebel culture during Chani rule and inspired by Auriellen and Frontroom's alternative culture, Nou Xaofé is practiced mainly by those ostracized due to rejecting traditional norms. Subcultures of Nou Xaofé include Hoqaydeon Grunge, Anarcho-Naturacore, Nouqu, Nomkh Industrial, Incongruous Scene, and Yeongist Post-punk, among others. ===Fashion=== The Ān Hāzāo is the national garment of Cascada, having developed from the cutting of curtains into two distinctive clothes and a connector: the Phāobà, a jacket; the Dǎjit, a sash used to tie the top and bottom parts of the Ān Hāzāo; and the Dàzíbà, a skirt. ''Ān Hāzāo'' translates literally as 'two clothes.' Ān Hāzāos were, and continue to be, traditionally made of repurposed curtains and therefore were usually beige. The color of clothing usually signaled one's status, with blue and gold being the most prestigious in ancient times; however, it has greatly diminished with the introduction of synthetic colors. {{Blurimage |file=Nook the goat.png |caption=Sketch of two Cascadans wearing traditional clothing. |align=left}} The Ān Hāzāo inspired other garments within Cascada's antiquity, primarily utilizing the Phāobà-Dàzíbà format. The format is seen from the Jóngxióng de to the Zhǔyìjuuija and even the newer Nouqu's Róng Nujiù. Despite their similar appearance, the clothing varied in usage and symbolism. The Zhǔyìjuuija, for example, was commonly worn by firefighters and made from heavy cotton and coated in small layers of snow. Alongside traditional clothing, Cascada has a variety of footwear, although they are largely divided between slippers (Tuō) and boots (Réncheu) and are made with the intention of keeping the feet warm and clean. Nou Xaofé fashion is heavily diversified and is influenced by human cultural movements. As core tenants, Nou Xaofé values "DIY-ism" and the ideas of individualism, promoting different appearances within a style. Hoqaydeon Grunge is anti-consumerist yet still upholds practical and utilitarian clothing seen in both modern Cascadan fashion and regular grunge; it is denoted for its accessories, which can double as self-defense items. Anarcho-Naturacore stems from anti-war, anti-capitalist, and anarchist ideals combined with natural harmonization; Anarcho-Naturacore's fashion tends to utilize anarchist symbols and red-black color schemes mixed in with greenish muted colors, flowers or grass, and worn-out clothing. A key motif of anarcho-naturacore is messy or unkempt fur. Nouqu fashion combines traditional Cascadan dresses and the newer, more universal notes of Nou Xaofé culture. Incongruous Scene upholds the philosophy of being conspicuous, anti-conformism, and individuality; colors within Incongruous Scene are vivid and bright, with complex patterns littering their clothing. People who dress in incongruous scene often paint their fur. War has shaped Cascadan fashion, and themes of it are not uncommonly seen represented in clothing. Modern Cascadan fashion has its roots in war, as seen through its scrappy, practical appearance. ===Cuisine=== Through the nation's long culinary past, it has developed a sophisticated and refined cuisine, especially fish. The harsh, cold winter seasons in Cascada forced agricultural gathering to be primarily concentrated in the spring and early autumn. This seasonal gathering included mushrooms, berries, and honey, along with some vegetables and fruits from orchards. Crops of rye, wheat, barley, and millet provided the ingredients for a plethora of breads, pancakes, pies, cereals, and beer. The mawfish and caviar are two of the most popular items of Cascadan cuisine, seen in a multitude of recipes. {{Blurimage |file=Assortment.png |caption=An assortment of foods, including caviar.}} Soups, hotpots, and stews are heavily demanded during the mid-autumn and winter. Despite varying in product, they are usually composed of fish and vegetables, with bread sometimes accompanying them. Trade has allowed for meats, noodles, and a greater variety of different fruits and vegetables, alongside rice, to enter Cascadan cuisine. Cascada is renowned for their native spices and its seasoning; wasabi and tāe native hāin xe have gained notoriety for their spiciness. Tea is the most popular drink within Cascada and is made from the jài yèlào bā, a close equivalent to camellia sinensis, the plant used to make tea, and is usually paired with milk, butter, or sugar. Popular alcoholic beverages within Cascada include Yóuknàh, Hóngsho Baihaang, and wine, although it is mainly reserved for the upper class. ===Art=== Cascadan art has historically been influenced by Jotean and Pyunchijoist ideas, which can be seen in the many traditional paintings, sculptures, ceramics and the performing arts. The interaction between Cascadan and human arts during the Oregómi Era was significant and instigated movements such as Dònglèi-zǚxaukeemism. The Cascadan tea ceremony, ōume, dùo jǔe, and nīng kēhada are also notable performing arts. Post-civil war, modern Cascadan art started to flourish in the 1980s and 1990s. Many Cascadan artists, amidst the chaos surrounding the inception of the Cascadan state and the widespread awareness of the Backrooms, gravitated toward the illogical and surrealist themes. Meanwhile, others sought to establish a sense of harmony between humanity and nature. Social issues and hopeful imagery emerged as prominent subjects during this period. The wars in Suburbia helped establish a militaristic style depicting acts of honor and framing war as honorable, but also sprung out a widespread anti-war countercultural movement. ===Internet culture=== Public internet in Cascada was inaccessible until 1997, but Cascadan government officials were given access to it by Aurielle in 1993 to assist with the coordination and planning of Auriellen forces. Cascadan internet culture is integrated with the Auriellen sphere; however, it significantly diverges from the nationalistic and satirically nationalist etiquette found in other areas of the Aurielospheric landscape. This deviation is primarily due to the absence of conflict between Cascada and Aurielle. Mùbōdo is the most popular and mainstream site, although Backchat is also widely used, within Cascada, where both people from Qǖ Xaofé and Nou Xaofé communities congregate. "Auriello-Cascadid" internet, as it is often dubbed, does not have memes as is traditionally seen in Auriellen or MEG internet, although they are mostly seen through old Auriellen ones from when the web had become publicly available. Internet jokes exist as more isolated inside jokes within specific communities and subcultures and remain incompatible with outsiders. This distinction is most commonly separated by age, generation, and Qǖ Xaofé and Nou Xaofé. {{Blurimage |file=Jinyi texts.png |caption=A conversation in Jinyi within Backchat.}} Despite inside jokes being the prevalent form of humor, outside of old Auriellen memes that continue to remain within the Cascadan sphere, some Cascadan internet-born memes are famous and used by most users, regardless of age or community, which are shībaishā (lit.; loser) and Chāohyeimojikons. Shībaishā is a character created by Cascadan artist Hong Xuefeng. This character mockingly represents an indoor loser who is excessively nationalistic but is incapable of taking real action for his nation, choosing instead to argue online. Originally intended to critique Suburbian nationalists, the term has since evolved into a general insult. Chāohyeimojikons are a wide range of emoticons influenced by Auriellen surrealism and initially used to depict Frontrooms-styled rage comics prior to becoming a way to express feelings, reactions, jokes, and cultural references. Generally, what occurs on the internet is considered "lesser" (nèi pin) than what occurs in reality. Subjects that are not from news sources or are less formal are trivialized or mocked and only taken seriously by their respective communities. Many within Cascada consider the online world as being "not real" or "not significant," even if the line between reality and the online world is blurred. Such apathy has led to cases of preventable suicides and has put into serious question the actual care for mental health in Cascada and its culture. Furthermore, it has also facilitated the expansion of extremist factions, such as the Black league, and prostitution rings. ===Influence=== "Cascadanization" refers to the influence of Cascada on other nations, usually through their popular culture, technology, cuisine, economic practices, and etiquette. Observed since the founding of Surzhou, it has since become the primary soft power method chosen, contrasting the soft-hard mixed power process of "Auriellenization," of Cascada and has been most prevelant after the Territorial Expansion Act, wider Cascadan accessibility to the internet, Auriellen-led globalization, and the creation of ASCIC. Rǘ ciá ciá (television world in Jinyi; Cascadan television) incorporates a wide variety of Cascadan entertainment, from television, radio, and the internet, and is a large media market within the broader Backrooms. Rǘ ciá ciá serves as one of the primary ways the international community observes Cascada's fashion, scenery, costumes, and daily life, just behind the newspaper and its adjacent xù ze (small paper), which are small stories usually found in the corner of newspapers. The Cascadan Dāogungsi, meanwhile, represents the industrial aspect of the Cascadanization, encompassing sectors from defense to electronics and machinery. Furries, while otherwise a generic subculture in the Frontrooms, have been completely recontextualized by the existence of Cascadans and now are the antithetical brother of Peíha, a Cascadan subculture defined by an interest in humans and dressing up like them. Furries, known as xàuwái in Cascada, describe people, often Cascadophiles, with an intense and at times obsessive interest in Cascadan culture. Furries have been labeled as a neo-nationalistic and pro-imperialist movement employed as a form of psychological warfare by the governments of Tolistan, Devysory, CHF, Ruphis, and Navia. ==Relations== Cascada is considered a regional power in international politics, but has been considered a great power in Level 330.1, Level 797, and Level 264. The nation is a member of VSESA, GG7, and the Development and Cooperation Economic Forum (DCEF). Cascada has been involved in both regional and international diplomatic missions and maintains strong ties with VSESA members, notably Aurielle and Telaso. The Duchy actively partakes in humanitarian missions, and more recently VSESA operations, making them an active and notable participant in VSESA. |-| Aurielle = Official relations between Cascada and the Republic of Aurielle on a diplomatic level were initiated in 1983 after Cascada had established itself as a unified duchy after years of being split into various different states. However, ties with Aurielle date back to 1926, when Persan explorer Marcus Agosti discovered Level 107 and initiated the Persan-Zuhoru alliance. During the various conflicts Aurielle's predecessors engaged in, Xiangzhou normalized relations with each emerging group. Aurielle supported and deployed forces to assist the republican effort in the Chani Civil War. Since then, Auriellen-Cascadan relations have been strong, and Cascada is one of the chief allies of Aurielle, being part of both VSESA and the GG7. There remains strong appreciation, both cultural and diplomatic, between the two nations, and according to the Auriellen Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Cascada is a staunch ally and one of the strongest partners in the Backrooms. Aurielle intervened in the Cascadan Conflict, and Cascada was one of the most important assets during the Korthie Casiagen War. |-| M.E.G. = Relations between MEG and Cascada have been historically hostile, majorly due to a lack of exposure to MEG outside of conflict. The two groups have no formal diplomatic relations and instead have adopted indirect diplomatic arrangements using neutral parties. The invasion of Aurielle by MEG and its coalition reinforced the pro-Auriellen stance of the Cascadan state toward MEG. This situation, coupled with the absence of efforts from either party to improve relations, has maintained the existing status quo. The source of hostilities outside of the ones stated beforehand is the alleged MEG support for the Poppies and the Masked Maidens during the Cascadan Emergency, alongside the conspiracy that ZETA is affiliated with the MEG. Cascadan support for Aurielle during the Korthie Casiagen War has also worsened and annihilated any chance of positive relations. |-| A.E.G. = Relations are recent but have gone through radical change. Starting with the AEG's Day of Dying Flames, Cascada had included the AEG in its list of Group Sponsors of Violence but continued to remain neutral in an attempt to avoid direct confrontation with them. The attack in Tianjia in 2019 raised concerns over AEG involvement, later confirmed in a raid on a AEG-operated FOB. Cascada attempted to diplomatically end the conflict but failed, leading to the Cascadan Conflict. By the end of the Cascadan Conflict, the Partialist faction assumed diplomatic responsibility in the Daveron Agreement and accepted terms that positively influenced AEG-Cascadan relations. Despite social tensions and distrust, Cascada and AEG have positive diplomatic relations. The AEG supported Cascada during the Cascadan Emergency. |-| SNC = The stance of Cascada within SNC is divided between pro-negotiation and hostiles, although it is largely dominated by those hostile to Cascada. Relations between Suburbia and Cascada began when Cascada invaded Greater Devysroy in 1997 and largely defined Suburbia's position of hostility against the Duchy. Suburbian nations condemned the Cascadan annexation of Greater Devysroy and hinted at military action to expel Cascada from the territory, but its efforts were hamstrung by the Atregoan insurgency in Kastenie. The situation worsened after Cascada invaded Clavis and Devysroy to reinstate the its Cascadan-aligned leaders. With the establishment of the SNC and the decline of the Atregoan insurgency, greater emphasis was placed on Hannamo. The Kharatan Summit set in stone Cascadan-SNC relations and originated the Suburbian-Cascadan conflict. The Gierenian CKH paramilitary group initiated a campaign of terror, assisted by the authorities in Clavis and Devysroy assigned with liberating their respective states from Cascadan rule. Cascada invaded and engaged in counterinsurgency, but they eventually withdrew, increasing hopes for a total Cascadan defeat in the future among the SNC. Distrust between Cascadan and the SNC heavily increased in the wake of the Serebryakov Massacre and the Tolistani killings, both perpetrated by Cascada, although no connections were formed. Relations worsened as tensions between Tolistan and Cascada exploded into the 2003 South Tolistan War. SNC Resolution 219 called for Cascada's immediate withdrawal, but they refused. Amidst the deteriorating situation, Anthra reconsidered the position of the SNC and began a process of peace with Cascada, marking the first major improvement in the SNC-Cascadan relations. However, a period of war primarily between Cascada, Clavis, and Tolistan destroyed any progress. Tolistan, CHF, and Clavis invaded Cascada in March 2014, starting the largest war between Cascada and the SNC. The SNC-Cascadan War resulted in the most significant political development of SNC-Cascadan relations, as the Kankaanrainen Treaties facilitated peace with Cascada which resulted in peace with Kastenie, Mechta, and Albazine, alongside improved relations with Dzofar and Kada. |-| Backrooms Red Cross & Backrooms Health Organisation = The Backrooms Red Cross, the Health Organisation, and the Duchy of Cascada entered formal diplomatic ties in 1991 as part of the Riumen Treaty in efforts to strengthen Cascada's health and expand its international position. Cascada is part of the Red Cross' International Safety and Medical Committee (ISMC) and provides a significant amount of funding to the Red Cross' missions. The nation has been involved in various Red Cross-mandated rescue operations and escort operations and also hosts a large Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority (BHPA) center in Level 107. Cascadan scientists and doctors often share discoveries with the BHO and Red Cross. In 2015, with mental health becoming a heavily scrutinized topic within Cascada, the BHO kickstarted a mission to implement the Liminal Health and Wellbeing Initiative within Cascada. Red Cross and BHO involvement in Cascada's healthcare systems has heavily increased over the years. |-| Garden of Ncipetheraz & Tehyovikwozhu = Ncipetherazi-Cascadan relations have long since been close; they have been partners and enjoyed cordial relations since Cascadan unification. The two nations became closer following the Cascadan settlement of Level 79 and the ensuing support of it by Ncipetheraz and its cultural colony in Ncipetheraz, Tehyovikwozhu. Both nations are regarded as ancient cradles of civilization within the Backrooms, but Ncipetherazi-Cascadan relations did not begin until the Zaamtāon Period, when Xiangzhouan scholars were sent to study in Ncipetheraz to learn about its culture, economy, and military; among these scholars was the prominent pro-unionist Zhou Xuegang, who later used Ncipetherazi's Vizhuism as a foundation for Zhùjì. As Cascada emerged, the two nations exhibited many similarities: both were ethnocratic, deeply religious, historically rich, and culturally alike in certain aspects. Under these conditions the two nations grew close, and the Duchy became a large importer of Ncipetherazi reforms and systems. Relations deteriorated under Dei Huitynl's leadership, especially with the passing of the Territorial Expansion Act, as Ncipetherazi deemed it a reverberation of militarist philosophy. Despite their apparent dislike for Cascada's government, their invasion of Greater Devysroy witnessed a shift in strategic relations. Cascada became a critical tool to curb Ruphis's influence within Suburbia and supported it by providing Cascada with a diplomatic foundation to solidify their claim. In 2001, Tehyovikwozhu agreed to clandestinely hold Cascadan military assets intended to arm and supply pro-democratic fighters in Gierenia outside of Ncipetherazi approval, later reactivated in 2014 to greatly hinder Gierenian efforts in the SNC-Cascadan war. Direct Ncipetherazi influence on Rauhallinen politics also played a crucial role in facilitating the Hvidstrup Treaty of Peace between Cascada and Rauhallinen. Sylvainist thought has developed as a political faction among progressive Cascadans, though this ideology remains largely isolated and lacks widespread popularity.
